Detailed_Business_Rule: The CBADMCDJ batch job is submitted under accounting code AWSCODR with notify recipient set to the submitter's user ID via &SYSUID. The job is routed to class A with hold-queue message class H and full job log and message printing (MSGLEVEL=(1,1)). Maximum CPU time is set to 1440 minutes (24 hours) to accommodate first-time CSD population for very large resource groups. This is a configuration job that registers CICS resources, not a typical data-processing batch.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //CBADMCDJ JOB (COBOL),'AWSCODR',CLASS=A,MSGCLASS=H,MSGLEVEL=(1,1),\n//         NOTIFY=&SYSUID,TIME=1440
Review_Comments: Hardcoded accounting string AWSCODR - externalize via job parameter; hardcoded TIME=1440 minute CPU limit - review under cloud SLA model (serverless functions have inherent timeouts)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defines a JCL symbolic parameter HLQ with the literal value AWS.M2.CARDDEMO. This high-level qualifier is later substituted into the LIBRARY DSNAME for the COM2DOLL load library at &HLQ..LOADLIB. Externalising the HLQ as a SET allows reuse of the JCL across environments only by editing this single line.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //   SET HLQ=AWS.M2.CARDDEMO
Review_Comments: Hardcoded HLQ AWS.M2.CARDDEMO - externalize via job parameter or AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store

Detailed_Business_Rule: The job invokes the IBM CICS-supplied offline utility DFHCSDUP to read and write resource definitions in the CICS System Definition (CSD) file. Read-write mode is selected so the job can both list existing definitions and add new ones. Page size is 60 lines per print page; compatibility mode is disabled (NOCOMPAT) to use the latest CSD format. The 0M region size requests maximum available virtual storage.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //STEP1   EXEC PGM=DFHCSDUP,REGION=0M,\n//          PARM='CSD(READWRITE),PAGESIZE(60),NOCOMPAT'
Review_Comments: DFHCSDUP utility has no AWS Glue / ECS equivalent - in cloud target, CICS resource definitions become Spring Boot @Component bean registrations or service-mesh routing rules; this entire step is replaced, not migrated

Input_File_Layout: N/A (PDSE load library containing DFHCSDUP module)
Detailed_Business_Rule: Specifies the load library that contains the DFHCSDUP utility executable. The DSN points to the CICS Transaction Server 5.6 supplied SDFHLOAD library shared across the LPAR. DISP=SHR allows concurrent access from multiple jobs.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //STEPLIB  DD  DSN=OEM.CICSTS.V05R06M0.CICS.SDFHLOAD,DISP=SHR
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN OEM.CICSTS.V05R06M0.CICS.SDFHLOAD - AWS Glue needs a connection param; CICS infrastructure has no cloud equivalent - replace with Spring Boot bean registration / AWS Cloud Map

Detailed_Business_Rule: Allocates the CICS System Definition file (CSD VSAM RRDS) as input/output for DFHCSDUP. All DEFINE, DELETE, and LIST directives in the SYSIN deck operate against this dataset. DISP=SHR allows the running CICS region to continue using the CSD while this job modifies it.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //DFHCSD   DD  UNIT=SYSDA,DISP=SHR,DSN=OEM.CICSTS.DFHCSD
Review_Comments: Hardcoded DSN OEM.CICSTS.DFHCSD - AWS Glue needs a connection param; CICS infrastructure has no cloud equivalent - replace with Spring Boot bean registration / AWS Cloud Map

Detailed_Business_Rule: Allocates the operator output stream OUTDD to SYSOUT class * so that any DFHCSDUP operator-mode messages are routed to the JES spool. This DD is required for some DFHCSDUP control statements but receives no output for typical DEFINE/LIST jobs.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //OUTDD    DD  SYSOUT=*

Detailed_Business_Rule: Allocates the SYSPRINT DD to SYSOUT class * for DFHCSDUP's diagnostic listing and message output. After the job runs, this stream contains success or failure of every DEFINE directive and the formatted output of any LIST directives.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //SYSPRINT DD  SYSOUT=*

Detailed_Business_Rule: Declares the in-stream control deck for DFHCSDUP with SYMBOLS=JCLONLY which restricts symbolic substitution to JCL symbols only (e.g., &HLQ). All subsequent control cards through the /* delimiter constitute the directive deck.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: //SYSIN    DD  *,SYMBOLS=JCLONLY

Detailed_Business_Rule: Commented-out DELETE GROUP(CARDDEMO) statement intended as a re-run safeguard. When the job is re-executed against a CSD that already contains the CARDDEMO group, this line must be uncommented first to remove the prior definitions, otherwise DFHCSDUP will issue duplicate-resource warnings. This is an operator-managed manual step prone to error.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: * DELETE GROUP(CARDDEMO)
Review_Comments: Manual re-run safeguard via commented-out DELETE - in cloud target, registration is idempotent by design (Spring Boot beans, AWS Cloud Map services); no manual uncomment step needed

Detailed_Business_Rule: Registers the application load library COM2DOLL within group CARDDEMO. The DSNAME is constructed by substituting the JCL symbol &HLQ to produce AWS.M2.CARDDEMO.LOADLIB. CICS uses this library as the source of executable load modules for every PROGRAM defined in the same group.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: DEFINE LIBRARY(COM2DOLL) GROUP(CARDDEMO)\n                DSNAME01(&HLQ..LOADLIB)
Review_Comments: Hardcoded HLQ AWS.M2.CARDDEMO - externalize via job parameter or AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store

Detailed_Business_Rule: Registers the BMS mapset COSGN00M (LOGIN SCREEN) with the CICS region under group CARDDEMO. The mapset binds the BMS presentation layer source (COSGN00.bms) to the runtime so transactions can use SEND MAP and RECEIVE MAP against this screen layout. The 3270 screen format is loaded into CICS map storage at INSTALL time.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: DEFINE MAPSET(COSGN00M)   GROUP(CARDDEMO)\n       DESCRIPTION(LOGIN SCREEN)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Duplicate registration of BMS mapset COSGN00M (LOGIN SCREEN) - the same mapset was already defined earlier in this same DFHCSDUP run. DFHCSDUP will accept the second DEFINE but emit a duplicate-resource warning; the second definition silently overrides the first. Indicates the same physical BMS map serves both the Account and Card contexts in CardDemo.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: DEFINE MAPSET(COSGN00M)   GROUP(CARDDEMO)\n       DESCRIPTION(LOGIN SCREEN)
Review_Comments: Duplicate DEFINE - same resource defined twice in same group; consolidate before migration

Detailed_Business_Rule: Registers BMS mapset COACT00S (ACCOUNT MENU) with the CICS region under group CARDDEMO. However, no source mapset file matching COACT00S exists in app/bms/ - the resource is registered but no compiled mapset module backs it. Any transaction that issues SEND MAP or RECEIVE MAP against this mapset will fail at runtime with a MAPFAIL condition.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: DEFINE MAPSET(COACT00S)   GROUP(CARDDEMO)\n       DESCRIPTION(ACCOUNT MENU)
Review_Comments: HIGH SEVERITY: Source mapset COACT00S missing from app/bms/ - registration cannot be honored at runtime; obtain source or remove DEFINE before deployment

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defines CICS transaction identifier CCDM bound to program COADM00C for the Admin Menu functionality, with task data above the line (TASKDATAL=ANY) for 31-bit addressing. End users invoke this transaction by typing CCDM on the CICS panel; CICS locates the bound program in group CARDDEMO and dispatches control. The bound program COADM00C has no source in app/cbl/, so this transaction will fail at runtime.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: DEFINE TRANSACTION(CCDM) GROUP(CARDDEMO)\n                PROGRAM(COADM00C) TASKDATAL(ANY)
Review_Comments: HIGH SEVERITY: Source program COADM00C.cbl missing from app/cbl/ - registration cannot be honored at runtime; obtain source or remove DEFINE before deployment; Multi-program CALL chain via TRANSID dispatching - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary in target architecture

Detailed_Business_Rule: Issues a verification listing of every resource currently defined in group CARDDEMO after all preceding DEFINE directives have run. The listing is written to SYSPRINT and serves as both an audit trail and an installer-confirmation artifact that all resources are present and have the expected attributes.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: LIST   GROUP(CARDDEMO)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Closes the SYSIN in-stream control deck with /* and ends the job stream with the // delimiter. JES2 returns control to the spool and posts job completion. Operator review of SYSPRINT and any non-zero return codes from DFHCSDUP is the manual operational follow-up.

The COSGN00C COBOL program is the CICS pseudo-conversational Signon screen for the CardDemo application. It is invoked under transaction CC00 and validates user credentials against the USRSEC VSAM file before transferring control via XCTL to either the admin menu (COADM01C) or the user main menu (COMEN01C). The program copies COCOM01Y for the common communication area, CSUSR01Y for the user-security record, and CSDAT01Y for date/time formatting.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.\nPROGRAM-ID. COSGN00C.\nAUTHOR.     AWS.

Input_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Top-level routing paragraph. Clears the error flag, the working message, and the screen error message line. Determines whether this is a first-time entry (no commarea) or a re-entry from a previous pseudo-conversation, then dispatches based on the attention identifier (Enter, PF3, or any other key).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MAIN-PARA.
    SET ERR-FLG-OFF TO TRUE
    MOVE SPACES TO WS-MESSAGE
                   ERRMSGO OF COSGN0AO

Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry (when no commarea has been received), the screen output area is cleared to low-values and the cursor is positioned on the User ID input field by setting its length indicator to -1. The empty signon screen is then sent to the user.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF EIBCALEN = 0
    MOVE LOW-VALUES TO COSGN0AO
    MOVE -1       TO USERIDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: On re-entry, when the user pressed the Enter key, the program performs the PROCESS-ENTER-KEY paragraph to validate user identifier and password. Validation outcomes are stored in the error flag and message-area fields for redisplay. If both fields pass validation, the program proceeds to authenticate against the User Security file; otherwise the screen is redisplayed with the specific error message and the cursor positioned at the offending field.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHENTER\n    PERFORM PROCESS-ENTER-KEY

Detailed_Business_Rule: On re-entry, when the user pressed PF3 to exit, the program moves the standard "Thank You" exit message into the working storage and exits the session. Control then returns to the calling region with no preserved state beyond the goodbye message. This path is the user-initiated logoff entry point.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHPF3\n    MOVE CCDA-MSG-THANK-YOU        TO WS-MESSAGE\n    PERFORM SEND-PLAIN-TEXT

Detailed_Business_Rule: On re-entry, when the user pressed any unsupported key (e.g., PF1-PF2, PF4-PF24, PA1-PA3), the error flag is set, the standard "Invalid key pressed." message is loaded, and the screen is redisplayed. This guard prevents users from triggering unintended behaviors via unmapped function keys. Only Enter and PF3 are valid on the sign-on screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'Y'                       TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE CCDA-MSG-INVALID-KEY      TO WS-MESSAGE
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Issues a CICS RETURN with TRANSID=CC00 and the commarea, establishing the pseudo-conversational pattern. CICS will re-invoke this program when the user submits the next screen interaction. The commarea preserves user identity, the from-program/from-tranid context, and any error state across the conversation.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RETURN
          TRANSID (WS-TRANID)
          COMMAREA (CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
          LENGTH(LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Receives the screen input from the COSGN00 mapset (map COSGN0A). User-entered values for the User ID field (USERIDI) and the Password field (PASSWDI) are populated into the symbolic input map structure. Response and reason codes are captured for error trapping.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RECEIVE
          MAP('COSGN0A')
          MAPSET('COSGN00')
          RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
          RESP2(WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC.

Detailed_Business_Rule: If the User ID input is blank or low-values, the error flag is set, the user is prompted with "Please enter User ID ...", and the cursor is positioned on the User ID field. The signon screen is re-sent with the error.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN USERIDI OF COSGN0AI = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
    MOVE 'Y'      TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Please enter User ID ...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO USERIDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: If the Password input is blank or low-values, the error flag is set, the user is prompted with "Please enter Password ...", and the cursor is positioned on the Password field. The signon screen is re-sent with the error.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN PASSWDI OF COSGN0AI = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
    MOVE 'Y'      TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Please enter Password ...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO PASSWDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: Uppercases both the entered user identifier and password using the COBOL intrinsic FUNCTION UPPER-CASE so that login is case-insensitive. The uppercased values are stored in working-storage and the user identifier is also propagated to the communication area for downstream programs.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(USERIDI OF COSGN0AI) TO
                WS-USER-ID
                CDEMO-USER-ID
MOVE FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(PASSWDI OF COSGN0AI) TO
                WS-USER-PWD

Detailed_Business_Rule: If both User ID and Password validation passed (no error flag set), the program proceeds to authenticate against the User Security file. The lookup uses the entered user identifier as the read key. A successful authentication populates the common communication area with the user's role code and routes the user to the appropriate menu; a failure produces a credential-error message.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IF NOT ERR-FLG-ON\n    PERFORM READ-USER-SEC-FILE\nEND-IF.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Populates the screen header (title, transaction-id, program name, current date, current time, APPLID, SYSID), copies the prepared error or info message into the screen output area, and renders the sign-on screen. The header fields are computed once per pseudo-conversational round-trip. Cursor is positioned at the User ID input field for the user's first interaction.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS SEND
          MAP('COSGN0A')
          MAPSET('COSGN00')
          FROM(COSGN0AO)
          ERASE
          CURSOR
END-EXEC.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sends a plain-text message (no map) to the terminal with ERASE and FREEKB so the keyboard is re-enabled, then issues an unconditional CICS RETURN to terminate the conversation. Used as the PF3 exit path to display the "Thank You" message.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS SEND TEXT
          FROM(WS-MESSAGE)
          LENGTH(LENGTH OF WS-MESSAGE)
          ERASE
          FREEKB
END-EXEC.
EXEC CICS RETURN
END-EXEC.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Captures the current system date and time using FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE and formats them as MM/DD/YY and HH:MM:SS for display on the screen header. Also populates the screen title, transaction-id, program-name, APPLID (CICS region identifier) and SYSID (system identifier) for diagnostic visibility.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE  TO WS-CURDATE-DATA
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MONTH       TO WS-CURDATE-MM
MOVE WS-CURDATE-DAY         TO WS-CURDATE-DD
MOVE WS-CURDATE-YEAR(3:2)   TO WS-CURDATE-YY

Input_File_Layout: SEC-USR-ID(PIC X(8))|SEC-USR-FNAME(PIC X(20))|SEC-USR-LNAME(PIC X(20))|SEC-USR-PWD(PIC X(8))|SEC-USR-TYPE(PIC X(1))|SEC-USR-FILLER(PIC X(23))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the User Security VSAM KSDS file using the entered user identifier as the key. The 80-byte record contains user ID, first name, last name, password, user type code (A=admin, U=regular user), and a 23-byte filler. Response and reason codes drive the downstream branch logic for the authentication outcome.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (WS-USRSEC-FILE)
     INTO      (SEC-USER-DATA)
     LENGTH    (LENGTH OF SEC-USER-DATA)
     RIDFLD    (WS-USER-ID)
     KEYLENGTH (LENGTH OF WS-USER-ID)
     RESP      (WS-RESP-CD)
     RESP2     (WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC.
Review_Comments: Plaintext password storage in USRSEC VSAM file (CSUSR01Y field SEC-USR-PWD PIC X(8)) - HIGH security risk; modernization target should hash with bcrypt/argon2 and migrate to encrypted RDS or DynamoDB store

Input_File_Layout: SEC-USR-ID(PIC X(8))|SEC-USR-FNAME(PIC X(20))|SEC-USR-LNAME(PIC X(20))|SEC-USR-PWD(PIC X(8))|SEC-USR-TYPE(PIC X(1))|SEC-USR-FILLER(PIC X(23))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On a successful read of the User Security record (response code NORMAL), the program proceeds to compare the stored password against the user-supplied password. If the comparison succeeds, the user's role code (A=admin, U=regular) is loaded into the communication area and the user is routed forward; if not, an "Invalid Password" error message is set and the sign-on screen is redisplayed for retry.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD\n    WHEN 0\n        IF SEC-USR-PWD = WS-USER-PWD

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: When the entered password matches the stored password, the communication area is initialized with this program's transaction (CC00), program name (COSGN00C), authenticated user identifier, user type code (A or U), and program context set to zero. This context is propagated to the next program.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE WS-TRANID    TO CDEMO-FROM-TRANID
MOVE WS-PGMNAME   TO CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM
MOVE WS-USER-ID   TO CDEMO-USER-ID
MOVE SEC-USR-TYPE TO CDEMO-USER-TYPE
MOVE ZEROS        TO CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: If the authenticated user has type code A (admin), control is transferred via XCTL to the admin-menu program COADM01C, passing the populated commarea. The current pseudo-conversation ends and a fresh one begins under COADM01C.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-USRTYP-ADMIN
     EXEC CICS XCTL
       PROGRAM ('COADM01C')
       COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
     END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Otherwise the user has type code U (regular user) and control is transferred via XCTL to the user main-menu program COMEN01C, passing the populated commarea. The current pseudo-conversation ends and a fresh one begins under COMEN01C.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
ELSE
     EXEC CICS XCTL
       PROGRAM ('COMEN01C')
       COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
     END-EXEC
END-IF
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: When the user record was found but the entered password does not match, the message "Wrong Password. Try again ..." is displayed on the screen, the cursor is positioned on the Password field, and the signon screen is re-sent.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
ELSE
    MOVE 'Wrong Password. Try again ...' TO
                                       WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO PASSWDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: When the User Security read returns response code 13 (NOTFND), the entered user identifier does not exist on file. The error flag is set, the message "User not found. Try again ..." is displayed, the cursor is positioned on the User ID field, and the signon screen is re-sent.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN 13
    MOVE 'Y'      TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'User not found. Try again ...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO USERIDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: Any other CICS response code from the User Security read (e.g., I/O error, file not open, security violation) is trapped. The error flag is set, the message "Unable to verify the User ..." is displayed, the cursor is positioned on the User ID field, and the signon screen is re-sent. The actual response code is not surfaced to the user nor logged for operations.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'Y'      TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Unable to verify the User ...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO USERIDL OF COSGN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-SIGNON-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Generic file error handler discards diagnostic detail (RESP/RESP2 not surfaced) - replace with structured logging that emits CloudWatch metrics and full error context in cloud target

Detailed_Business_Rule: The COACTVWC COBOL program accepts and processes the Account View request under transaction CAVW. Given an Account ID it looks up the Card cross-reference (CXACAIX) by account, then reads the Account master (ACCTDAT) and Customer master (CUSTDAT) and renders the consolidated detail on the COACTVW BMS screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID.
    COACTVWC.
DATE-WRITTEN.
    May 2022.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Establishes a CICS abend trap with label ABEND-ROUTINE so that any unhandled abend (e.g., AEY9 program-not-found, ASRA storage error, AKCT timeout) is intercepted by this program rather than propagating to the CICS region's default handler. The trap allows the program to send a friendly error screen and produce a structured diagnostic before terminating. This is a defensive pattern applied at every program entry point.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: EXEC CICS HANDLE ABEND\n          LABEL(ABEND-ROUTINE)\nEND-EXEC
Review_Comments: HANDLE ABEND with local label - replace with structured exception handling and observability emit (CloudWatch metric/log) in target architecture

Detailed_Business_Rule: Initializes the working-storage scratchpad (CC-WORK-AREA, WS-MISC-STORAGE, WS-COMMAREA), stores the program's transaction-id and program-id for downstream display, and clears any leftover state from a prior session. Initialization is performed on every entry to ensure no stale data leaks across pseudo-conversational iterations. Memory is reset to known defaults before any business logic executes.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
INITIALIZE CC-WORK-AREA
           WS-MISC-STORAGE
           WS-COMMAREA
MOVE LIT-THISTRANID       TO WS-TRANID
SET WS-RETURN-MSG-OFF  TO TRUE

Input_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry (no commarea) or entry from the main menu without re-enter context, the entire commarea and program private commarea are initialized to default values. Otherwise the program copies the inbound DFHCOMMAREA into the commarea and the local program commarea (in two adjacent slices).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF EIBCALEN IS EQUAL TO 0
    OR (CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM = LIT-MENUPGM
    AND NOT CDEMO-PGM-REENTER)
   INITIALIZE CARDDEMO-COMMAREA
              WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
ELSE
   MOVE DFHCOMMAREA (1:LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)  TO
                     CARDDEMO-COMMAREA
   MOVE DFHCOMMAREA(LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA + 1:
                    LENGTH OF WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA ) TO
                     WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Performs the common YYYY-STORE-PFKEY paragraph (sourced from the CSSTRPFY copybook), which captures and remaps the attention identifier (the key the user pressed) into program-specific flags such as CCARD-AID-ENTER, CCARD-AID-PFK01-PFK24, and CCARD-AID-PA1-PA3. This indirection lets the main dispatcher branch on a single program-local enum rather than the raw CICS attention identifier byte. The mapping is reused by every screen-driven program in CardDemo.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: PERFORM YYYY-STORE-PFKEY\n   THRU YYYY-STORE-PFKEY-EXIT

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defaults the PF-key flag to invalid then re-enables it only when the AID is Enter or PF3. Any other key forces the AID back to Enter so the program processes a screen refresh instead of an unsupported function.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SET PFK-INVALID TO TRUE
IF CCARD-AID-ENTER OR
   CCARD-AID-PFK03
   SET PFK-VALID TO TRUE
END-IF
IF PFK-INVALID
   SET CCARD-AID-ENTER TO TRUE
END-IF

WHEN CCARD-AID-PFK03

Detailed_Business_Rule: On fresh entry to the program (program context flag = 0), the program performs 1000-SEND-MAP to display the empty Account View screen prompting the user for an Account ID. No data has been fetched yet, so the screen shows blank fields. The program then exits via pseudo-conversational return, awaiting the user's input.

Detailed_Business_Rule: On re-entry (PGM-CONTEXT=1), the program processes user input via 2000-PROCESS-INPUTS. If validation fails, re-display the screen with the error and return. If validation succeeds, perform 9000-READ-ACCT to fetch the account/customer data, re-display the populated screen, and return.

WHEN CDEMO-PGM-REENTER
     PERFORM 2000-PROCESS-INPUTS
        THRU 2000-PROCESS-INPUTS-EXIT
     IF INPUT-ERROR
        PERFORM 1000-SEND-MAP
           THRU 1000-SEND-MAP-EXIT

     ELSE

           THRU 9000-READ-ACCT-EXIT

           THRU 1000-SEND-MAP-EXIT
        GO TO COMMON-RETURN
     END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defensive catch-all branch for unexpected scenarios. Sets the abend culprit to this program, the abend code to 0001, the abend reason to spaces, the return message to "UNEXPECTED DATA SCENARIO", and displays a plain-text exit. Indicates the program received context that did not match any expected branch.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
     MOVE LIT-THISPGM    TO ABEND-CULPRIT
     MOVE '0001'         TO ABEND-CODE
     MOVE SPACES         TO ABEND-REASON
     MOVE 'UNEXPECTED DATA SCENARIO'
                         TO WS-RETURN-MSG

        THRU SEND-PLAIN-TEXT-EXIT

If validation or any read fails, the screen is redisplayed with an error message and the cursor positioned at the offending field. On success, the populated account-detail screen is rendered.

IF INPUT-ERROR

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF3 (exit), the program returns control to the calling program (or to the main menu if invoked standalone) via pseudo-conversational transfer. The current communication area is preserved so the calling program receives back any context it had passed in. No further work is done by this program; control flows out.

MOVE WS-RETURN-MSG     TO CCARD-ERROR-MSG
MOVE  CARDDEMO-COMMAREA    TO WS-COMMAREA

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF4 (clear screen), the program clears all input fields and re-renders the empty Account View screen. This is the user-initiated reset path, equivalent to starting over without exiting. The clear is destructive — any unsaved screen state is lost.

1000-SEND-MAP.

This guard rejects unintended keystrokes and informs the user which keys are valid. Only Enter, PF3, and PF4 are accepted on the Account View screen.

The CICS region drops the program's working storage and resumes execution at this same program when the next user keystroke arrives. Detailed_Business_Rule: Sets the BMS attribute byte for the Account ID input field, positions the cursor on the field flagged for input, and sets the field color: red if the account filter is invalid or blank, default otherwise. The info message field is colored dark for empty messages and neutral for populated info.

MOVE DFHBMFSE               TO ACCTSIDA OF CACTVWAI
EVALUATE TRUE
   WHEN FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK
   WHEN FLG-ACCTFILTER-BLANK
        MOVE -1             TO ACCTSIDL OF CACTVWAI
   WHEN OTHER
        MOVE -1             TO ACCTSIDL OF CACTVWAI
END-EVALUATE
MOVE DFHDFCOL               TO ACCTSIDC OF CACTVWAO
IF FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK
   MOVE DFHRED              TO ACCTSIDC OF CACTVWAO
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Receive screen input by mapping the COACTVW BMS map into the COACTVWI input area. Response and reason codes are captured for downstream error handling. If the user pressed CLEAR or no map data was sent, the receive returns a MAPFAIL response that the caller must handle.

MOVE LIT-THISMAPSET         TO CCARD-NEXT-MAPSET
MOVE LIT-THISMAP            TO CCARD-NEXT-MAP
SET  CDEMO-PGM-REENTER TO TRUE
EXEC CICS SEND MAP(CCARD-NEXT-MAP)
               MAPSET(CCARD-NEXT-MAPSET)
               FROM(CACTVWAO)
               CURSOR
               ERASE
               FREEKB
               RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the entered Account ID is non-blank and 11 digits. If blank, sets an error flag and the prompt "Please enter an Account ID.". If non-numeric or wrong length, sets an error flag and the prompt "Account ID must be 11 numeric digits.". The cursor is positioned on the Account ID field for correction.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 2100-RECEIVE-MAP
   THRU 2100-RECEIVE-MAP-EXIT
PERFORM 2200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS
   THRU 2200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS-EXIT
MOVE WS-RETURN-MSG  TO CCARD-ERROR-MSG
MOVE LIT-THISPGM    TO CCARD-NEXT-PROG

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the account master VSAM file using the validated Account ID as the key. On NORMAL response, the account record is loaded into the screen-display structure. On NOTFND, an "Account not found." error is set and the cursor is returned to the Account ID field. On any other response, a generic file-error message is set.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RECEIVE MAP(LIT-THISMAP)
          MAPSET(LIT-THISMAPSET)
          INTO(CACTVWAI)
          RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
          RESP2(WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: If the entered account ID is asterisk or spaces, normalize to low-values; otherwise pass through. Then perform field-level edit 2210-EDIT-ACCOUNT to enforce the 11-digit numeric rule. If the filter ends up blank, set the cross-field error flag NO-SEARCH-CRITERIA-RECEIVED.

SET INPUT-OK                  TO TRUE
SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-ISVALID    TO TRUE
IF  ACCTSIDI OF CACTVWAI = '*'
OR  ACCTSIDI OF CACTVWAI = SPACES
    MOVE LOW-VALUES           TO  CC-ACCT-ID
ELSE
    MOVE ACCTSIDI OF CACTVWAI TO  CC-ACCT-ID
END-IF
PERFORM 2210-EDIT-ACCOUNT
   THRU 2210-EDIT-ACCOUNT-EXIT

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the card cross-reference file using Account ID as the secondary key. The cross-reference returns the primary card number associated with the account. On NOTFND, the program records that no card is on file but continues without blocking the view (some account types are card-less). Other response codes trigger the generic file-error path.

IF CC-ACCT-ID   EQUAL LOW-VALUES
OR CC-ACCT-ID   EQUAL SPACES
   SET INPUT-ERROR           TO TRUE
   SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-BLANK  TO TRUE
   IF WS-RETURN-MSG-OFF
      SET WS-PROMPT-FOR-ACCT TO TRUE
   END-IF
   MOVE ZEROES       TO CDEMO-ACCT-ID

END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: If the account ID is non-numeric or all zeroes, set INPUT-ERROR, set the NOT-OK filter flag, copy the message "Account Filter must be a non-zero 11 digit number" to the return message, default the account to zero, and skip to the paragraph exit. Otherwise the account is valid and is moved to the commarea account ID with the ISVALID flag set.

IF CC-ACCT-ID  IS NOT NUMERIC
OR CC-ACCT-ID  EQUAL ZEROES
   SET INPUT-ERROR TO TRUE
   SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK TO TRUE
   IF WS-RETURN-MSG-OFF

                     TO WS-RETURN-MSG
   END-IF

ELSE

END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Coordinator paragraph that fetches three records: (1) the Card cross-reference by account via 9200-GETCARDXREF-BYACCT, (2) the account master via 9300-GETACCTDATA-BYACCT, and (3) the customer master via 9400-GETCUSTDATA-BYCUST. After each step it checks the not-found flag and exits early if the lookup failed.

   GO TO 9000-READ-ACCT-EXIT
END-IF
PERFORM 9300-GETACCTDATA-BYACCT
   THRU 9300-GETACCTDATA-BYACCT-EXIT
IF DID-NOT-FIND-ACCT-IN-ACCTDAT
   GO TO 9000-READ-ACCT-EXIT
END-IF
MOVE CDEMO-CUST-ID TO WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID
PERFORM 9400-GETCUSTDATA-BYCUST

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the Card Cross-Reference VSAM file via the alternate index path CXACAIX keyed by account ID. On a successful read (NORMAL response), the customer ID and primary card number are copied into the commarea fields CDEMO-CUST-ID and CDEMO-CARD-NUM for downstream use.

EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (LIT-CARDXREFNAME-ACCT-PATH)
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)
     KEYLENGTH (LENGTH OF WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)

Detailed_Business_Rule: When no cross-reference record is found for the supplied account ID, set the INPUT-ERROR and NOT-OK filter flags and build a diagnostic return message "Account: NN not found in Cross ref file. Resp:NN Reas:NN" via STRING concatenation.

WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK       TO TRUE

    'Account:' WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X ' not found in'
    ' Cross ref file.  Resp:' ERROR-RESP
    ' Reas:' ERROR-RESP2
    DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO WS-RETURN-MSG
    END-STRING

Sets error flags, populates the WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE template (operation name, file, RESP, RESP2), and copies it to the return message.     MOVE LIT-CARDXREFNAME-ACCT-PATH TO ERROR-FILE
    MOVE WS-RESP-CD                 TO ERROR-RESP
    MOVE WS-REAS-CD                 TO ERROR-RESP2
    MOVE WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WS-RETURN-MSG
*    PERFORM SEND-LONG-TEXT

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: When the customer master read returns NOTFND, set INPUT-ERROR and CUSTFILTER-NOT-OK flags and build a diagnostic return message "CustId:NN not found in customer master.Resp: NN REAS:NN". Indicates referential-integrity failure where the cross-reference points to a non-existent customer.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-CUSTFILTER-NOT-OK       TO TRUE
    STRING 'CustId:' WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID-X ' not found'
    ' in customer master.Resp: ' ERROR-RESP
    ' REAS:' ERROR-RESP2
    DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO WS-RETURN-MSG
    END-STRING
Review_Comments: Referential integrity gap - cross-reference points to missing customer; in target architecture enforce FK constraint or cascade delete

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Generic file-error handler for non-NORMAL non-NOTFND responses from the customer master read. Builds the standard WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE and copies it to the return message. SEND-LONG-TEXT is commented out.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-CUSTFILTER-NOT-OK                TO TRUE
    MOVE 'READ'                     TO ERROR-OPNAME
    MOVE LIT-CUSTFILENAME           TO ERROR-FILE
    MOVE WS-RESP-CD                 TO ERROR-RESP
    MOVE WS-REAS-CD                 TO ERROR-RESP2
    MOVE WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WS-RETURN-MSG

Detailed_Business_Rule: Plain-text exit path - sends WS-RETURN-MSG to the terminal with ERASE and FREEKB then issues unconditional CICS RETURN. Comment notes "Don't use in production" because it bypasses the BMS map and proper screen layout.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS SEND TEXT
          FROM(WS-RETURN-MSG)
          LENGTH(LENGTH OF WS-RETURN-MSG)
          ERASE
          FREEKB
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS RETURN
END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Production code includes "Don't use in production" debug exit path - remove or guard with environment flag in modernized code

Detailed_Business_Rule: Debug-only long-text exit path - sends WS-LONG-MSG (500-byte message buffer) to the terminal with ERASE and FREEKB then issues unconditional CICS RETURN. All callers of this paragraph are commented out, indicating debug code that was left in the program.

EXEC CICS SEND TEXT
          FROM(WS-LONG-MSG)
          LENGTH(LENGTH OF WS-LONG-MSG)
          ERASE

END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Abend handler invoked when an unhandled abend (e.g., AEY9 program-not-found, ASRA storage error) is intercepted by the registered abend trap. If no abend message has been set, defaults to "UNEXPECTED ABEND OCCURRED.". Records this program as the abend culprit, sends the abend data area to the screen, cancels the abend trap so the next abend will not loop back, and raises a hardcoded abend code 9999 to terminate the task. This terminal handler is the program's last line of defense before the CICS region issues a default abend dump.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ABEND-MSG EQUAL LOW-VALUES
   MOVE 'UNEXPECTED ABEND OCCURRED.' TO ABEND-MSG
END-IF
MOVE LIT-THISPGM       TO ABEND-CULPRIT
EXEC CICS SEND
                 FROM (ABEND-DATA)
                 LENGTH(LENGTH OF ABEND-DATA)
                 NOHANDLE
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS HANDLE ABEND
     CANCEL
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS ABEND
     ABCODE('9999')
END-EXEC
Review_Comments: EXEC CICS ABEND with hardcoded code 9999 - replace with structured exception handling and observability emit (CloudWatch metric/log)

Detailed_Business_Rule: The COACTUPC program processes Account Update requests under transaction CAUP. It performs READ-with-UPDATE locking against ACCTDAT and CUSTDAT, validates user input through 13 specialized field-edit subroutines (mandatory, alpha, alphanumeric, signed-decimal, US phone, US SSN, US state code, FICO score, state-zip, yes/no), confirms changes, and rewrites both records. This is the largest program in CardDemo at 4,236 lines.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.\nPROGRAM-ID.\n    COACTUPC.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Initialize working-storage scratchpad on every program entry: clear CC-WORK-AREA and WS-MISC-STORAGE to spaces/zeroes, store the program's transaction-id and program-id, and prepare the screen output area. This zeroing prevents stale data from a prior pseudo-conversational iteration from leaking into the current request. Initialization runs before any business logic.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: EXEC CICS HANDLE ABEND\n          LABEL(ABEND-ROUTINE)\nEND-EXEC
Review_Comments: HANDLE ABEND with local label - replace with structured exception handling and observability emit (CloudWatch metric/log) in target architecture

Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry or entry from main menu without re-enter context, initialize the commarea and the program private commarea, set program context to ENTER, and mark account details as not yet fetched. Otherwise, copy the inbound DFHCOMMAREA payload into the two adjacent commarea slices.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF EIBCALEN IS EQUAL TO 0
    OR (CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM = LIT-MENUPGM
    AND NOT CDEMO-PGM-REENTER)
   INITIALIZE CARDDEMO-COMMAREA
              WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
   SET CDEMO-PGM-ENTER TO TRUE
   SET ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED TO TRUE
ELSE
   MOVE DFHCOMMAREA (1:LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)  TO
                     CARDDEMO-COMMAREA
   MOVE DFHCOMMAREA(LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA + 1:
                    LENGTH OF WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA ) TO
                     WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defaults the PF-key flag to invalid then re-enables it only when the AID is one of: Enter, PF3 (exit), PF5 (save changes - only valid after edits OK), or PF12 (cancel - only valid when details have been fetched). Any other key forces the AID back to Enter.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SET PFK-INVALID TO TRUE
IF CCARD-AID-ENTER OR
   CCARD-AID-PFK03 OR
   (CCARD-AID-PFK05 AND ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED)
                   OR
   (CCARD-AID-PFK12 AND NOT ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED)
   SET PFK-VALID TO TRUE
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF3 (exit), the program issues a synchronization point to commit any pending VSAM updates (none expected at this point), then transfers control to either the calling program or the main menu. The explicit commit at exit time is unusual since the update path itself does not issue one — it normally relies on the implicit task-end commit. Defensive practice ensures any in-flight changes are persisted before the user navigates away.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN CCARD-AID-PFK03
     SET CCARD-AID-PFK03     TO TRUE
     ...
     EXEC CICS
          SYNCPOINT
     END-EXEC
     EXEC CICS XCTL
          PROGRAM (CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM)
          COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
     END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF4 (cancel pending changes), the program discards any unsaved input edits and re-renders the screen with the original (last-fetched) values. The user's edits are not persisted and the program returns to display-mode. This is the user-initiated rollback path before commit.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED
 AND CDEMO-PGM-ENTER
WHEN CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM   EQUAL LIT-MENUPGM
 AND NOT CDEMO-PGM-REENTER
     INITIALIZE WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
     PERFORM 3000-SEND-MAP THRU
             3000-SEND-MAP-EXIT
     SET CDEMO-PGM-REENTER        TO TRUE
     SET ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED TO TRUE
     GO TO COMMON-RETURN
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: After a successful or failed update operation, reset the search keys (account ID, private commarea, miscellaneous), redisplay the empty edit map, set re-enter context, and branch to common-return. This positions the user for a fresh search after each update cycle.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-AND-DONE
WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-FAILED
     INITIALIZE WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA
                WS-MISC-STORAGE
                CDEMO-ACCT-ID
     SET CDEMO-PGM-ENTER            TO TRUE
     PERFORM 3000-SEND-MAP THRU
             3000-SEND-MAP-EXIT
     SET CDEMO-PGM-REENTER          TO TRUE
     SET ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED   TO TRUE
     GO TO COMMON-RETURN
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Default branch when account details have been shown to the user. Process the user's input via 1000-PROCESS-INPUTS, decide the next action via 2000-DECIDE-ACTION (using flags set during edit), redisplay the screen via 3000-SEND-MAP, and branch to common-return for pseudo-conversational return.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
     PERFORM 1000-PROCESS-INPUTS
        THRU 1000-PROCESS-INPUTS-EXIT
     PERFORM 2000-DECIDE-ACTION
        THRU 2000-DECIDE-ACTION-EXIT
     PERFORM 3000-SEND-MAP
        THRU 3000-SEND-MAP-EXIT
     GO TO COMMON-RETURN
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF12 or any other unhandled key, the program sets the error flag, loads the standard "Invalid key pressed." message, and re-renders the screen. Only Enter, PF3, PF4, and PF5 are accepted on the Account Update screen. This guard ensures users do not trigger unintended actions via unmapped function keys.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE WS-RETURN-MSG     TO CCARD-ERROR-MSG
MOVE  CARDDEMO-COMMAREA    TO WS-COMMAREA
MOVE  WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA TO
       WS-COMMAREA(LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA + 1:
                    LENGTH OF WS-THIS-PROGCOMMAREA )
EXEC CICS RETURN
     TRANSID (LIT-THISTRANID)
     COMMAREA (WS-COMMAREA)
     LENGTH(LENGTH OF WS-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Wrapper that performs 1100-RECEIVE-MAP to receive screen input and 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS to validate every editable field. After validation, the next-program identifiers are stored in the screen carry-over fields so the subsequent iteration knows which workflow to continue. This wrapper is the single entry point into the input-processing pipeline.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 1100-RECEIVE-MAP
   THRU 1100-RECEIVE-MAP-EXIT
PERFORM 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS
   THRU 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS-EXIT
MOVE WS-RETURN-MSG  TO CCARD-ERROR-MSG
MOVE LIT-THISPGM    TO CCARD-NEXT-PROG

Detailed_Business_Rule: Receives the COACTUP screen and copies all 30+ input fields into the symbolic input area CACTUPAI. The ACUP-NEW-DETAILS structure is initialized to LOW-VALUES so each field can be individually populated below.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RECEIVE MAP(LIT-THISMAP)
          MAPSET(LIT-THISMAPSET)
          INTO(CACTUPAI)
          RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
          RESP2(WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC
INITIALIZE ACUP-NEW-DETAILS

Detailed_Business_Rule: Normalizes the entered account ID: if it is asterisk or spaces, store low-values; otherwise copy the input through. This applies to both the search key (CC-ACCT-ID) and the new-values structure (ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID-X).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF  ACCTSIDI OF CACTUPAI = '*'
OR  ACCTSIDI OF CACTUPAI = SPACES
    MOVE LOW-VALUES           TO CC-ACCT-ID
                                 ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID-X
ELSE
    MOVE ACCTSIDI OF CACTUPAI TO CC-ACCT-ID
                                 ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID-X
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: If account details have not yet been fetched (the user has not yet looked up an account), only the account ID needs validation - no field-level edits apply. Skip to the paragraph exit to short-circuit the rest of the receive logic.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IF ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED\n   GO TO 1100-RECEIVE-MAP-EXIT\nEND-IF
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Sub_Module: 1100-RECEIVE-MAP - Active Status, Credit Limit, Cash Limit
Detailed_Business_Rule: Each numeric input field (Credit Limit, Cash Limit, Current Balance, Cycle Credit, Cycle Debit) is conditionally parsed via FUNCTION TEST-NUMVAL-C to check format, then converted via FUNCTION NUMVAL-C into a packed-decimal numeric edit field. Asterisk or spaces in the input means "no change"; valid numerics populate the new-values record.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF FUNCTION TEST-NUMVAL-C(ACUP-NEW-CREDIT-LIMIT-X) = 0
   COMPUTE ACUP-NEW-CREDIT-LIMIT-N =
      FUNCTION NUMVAL-C(ACRDLIMI OF CACTUPAI)
ELSE
   CONTINUE
END-IF
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Detailed_Business_Rule: All other map input fields (Open Date, Expiry Date, Reissue Date, Group ID, Customer ID, Customer Name fields, Address, State, Zip, Country, Phone Numbers, SSN, DOB, EFT Account ID, FICO Score, Primary Card Holder Indicator, GovtIssued ID) are transferred to the ACUP-NEW-DETAILS structure with the same asterisk-or-space-means-no-change pattern. The structure feeds 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS for field-level validation.

IF  ADTOPENI OF CACTUPAI = '*'
OR  ADTOPENI OF CACTUPAI = SPACES
    MOVE LOW-VALUES           TO ACUP-NEW-OPEN-DATE
ELSE
    MOVE ADTOPENI OF CACTUPAI TO ACUP-NEW-OPEN-DATE
END-IF
(repeated for each of 30+ input fields)

Detailed_Business_Rule: When details have not yet been fetched, only validate the search key (Account ID) via 1210-EDIT-ACCOUNT, clear any old details from the in-memory cache, and exit early. This branch handles the initial-display path where the user has only entered the Account ID and pressed Enter to retrieve the record. Full edit validation is deferred until after the record has been fetched.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF  ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED
    PERFORM 1210-EDIT-ACCOUNT
       THRU 1210-EDIT-ACCOUNT-EXIT
    MOVE LOW-VALUES           TO ACUP-OLD-ACCT-DATA
    IF  FLG-ACCTFILTER-BLANK
        SET NO-SEARCH-CRITERIA-RECEIVED TO TRUE
    END-IF
    GO TO 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS-EXIT
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Performs 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW to detect whether any field has changed since the prior screen. If no changes are found, or changes are pending confirmation, or changes have already been applied, skip the field-level edit subroutines and jump to 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS-EXIT.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW
   THRU 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW-EXIT
IF  NO-CHANGES-FOUND
OR  ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED
OR  ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-AND-DONE
    MOVE LOW-VALUES           TO WS-NON-KEY-FLAGS
    GO TO 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Calls each specialized edit subroutine in sequence: 1220 for Active Status (Y/N), EDIT-DATE-CCYYMMDD for dates (Open, Expiry, Reissue, DOB), 1250 for signed decimals (Credit Limit, Cash Limit, Balance, Cycle Credit, Cycle Debit), 1265 for SSN, EDIT-DATE-OF-BIRTH for DOB validation, 1245 for FICO Score, 1275 for FICO range, 1225 for required alpha (First Name, Last Name, State, City, Country), 1235 for optional alpha (Middle Name), 1215 for mandatory text (Address Line 1), 1245 for numeric required (Zip, EFT), 1260 for US Phone (1 and 2), 1270 for State Code, 1280 for State-Zip cross-validation. Sets ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED if all edits pass.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 'Account Status'          TO WS-EDIT-VARIABLE-NAME
MOVE ACUP-NEW-ACTIVE-STATUS    TO WS-EDIT-YES-NO
PERFORM 1220-EDIT-YESNO
   THRU 1220-EDIT-YESNO-EXIT
...
(repeated for 17 business fields)

Detailed_Business_Rule: After individual State and Zip Code edits pass, performs cross-field validation 1280-EDIT-US-STATE-ZIP-CD to confirm the Zip Code's first 3 digits are valid for the selected US state. This guards against typo combinations like NY/90210 that individually pass but together violate USPS conventions. On mismatch, an error is raised and the cursor is positioned at the Zip Code field.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF  FLG-STATE-ISVALID
AND FLG-ZIPCODE-ISVALID
    PERFORM 1280-EDIT-US-STATE-ZIP-CD
       THRU 1280-EDIT-US-STATE-ZIP-CD-EXIT
END-IF
IF INPUT-ERROR
   CONTINUE
ELSE
   SET ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED TO TRUE
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Compares every account field in the new-values structure to the old-values structure (read at lookup time): account ID, active status (case-insensitive), current balance, credit limit, cash credit limit, open date, expiry date, reissue date, current cycle credit/debit, group ID. If all match, set NO-CHANGES-FOUND; otherwise set CHANGE-HAS-OCCURRED and skip to exit.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF  ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID-X         = ACUP-OLD-ACCT-ID-X
AND FUNCTION UPPER-CASE (ACUP-NEW-ACTIVE-STATUS) =
    FUNCTION UPPER-CASE (ACUP-OLD-ACTIVE-STATUS)
AND ACUP-NEW-CURR-BAL          = ACUP-OLD-CURR-BAL
...
    CONTINUE
ELSE
    SET CHANGE-HAS-OCCURRED   TO TRUE
    GO TO 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Manual change detection across 30+ fields - replace with JPA dirty-check or Liquibase change tracking; GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Compares every customer field in the new-values structure to the old-values structure: customer ID, name fields (case-insensitive trimmed), address lines, state, country, zip, phone numbers (3-segment each), SSN, government issued ID, DOB, EFT account ID, primary card holder flag, FICO score. If all match, set NO-CHANGES-DETECTED; otherwise set CHANGE-HAS-OCCURRED and skip to exit.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(FUNCTION TRIM(ACUP-NEW-CUST-ID-X))=
   FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(FUNCTION TRIM(ACUP-OLD-CUST-ID-X))
AND ...
    SET NO-CHANGES-DETECTED   TO TRUE
ELSE
    SET CHANGE-HAS-OCCURRED   TO TRUE
    GO TO 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW-EXIT
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: If account ID is low-values or spaces, set INPUT-ERROR and BLANK filter flag, set the user prompt for account ID, default the account to zeroes (both communication area and new-record), and exit early. This is the empty-input guard that prevents downstream lookups from running with an invalid key. The user is prompted to enter a valid Account ID before any further processing.

IF CC-ACCT-ID   EQUAL LOW-VALUES
OR CC-ACCT-ID   EQUAL SPACES
   SET INPUT-ERROR           TO TRUE
   SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-BLANK  TO TRUE

   MOVE ZEROES               TO CDEMO-ACCT-ID
                                ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID

END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: If the account ID is non-numeric or its numeric redefinition equals zeros, set INPUT-ERROR, populate WS-RETURN-MSG with "Account Number if supplied must be a 11 digit Non-Zero Number", default the commarea account to zeroes, and skip to exit. Otherwise the account is valid and is moved to the commarea with the ISVALID flag set.

MOVE CC-ACCT-ID              TO ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID
IF CC-ACCT-ID   IS NOT NUMERIC
OR CC-ACCT-ID-N EQUAL ZEROS
   SET INPUT-ERROR TO TRUE

    ' Non-Zero Number'

ELSE

END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reusable validator that any required free-text field must be non-blank and contain at least one printable character. If blank, set INPUT-ERROR, set the BLANK flag, build a context-aware return message "<Field Name> must be supplied." and skip to exit. Otherwise mark valid.

IF WS-EDIT-ALPHANUM-ONLY(1:WS-EDIT-ALPHANUM-LENGTH)

OR WS-EDIT-ALPHANUM-ONLY(1:WS-EDIT-ALPHANUM-LENGTH)

OR FUNCTION LENGTH(FUNCTION TRIM(

IF WS-EDIT-YES-NO             EQUAL LOW-VALUES
OR WS-EDIT-YES-NO             EQUAL SPACES
OR WS-EDIT-YES-NO             EQUAL ZEROS
