Detailed_Business_Rule: Reusable validator for optional alphanumeric fields. Empty value is acceptable; non-empty must be alphanumeric only.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: (optional INSPECT-based ALPHANUM check)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reusable validator that a required numeric field contains only digits 0-9 with optional leading sign. Empty value fails with "must be supplied"; invalid characters fail with "can only contain numbers."
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: (INSPECT-based numeric check)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reusable validator for signed-decimal monetary amounts in S9(10)V99 format. Verifies optional sign, digits, decimal point, and 2-digit fraction. Failure to parse produces "<Field Name> is not a valid signed number."
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IF FUNCTION TEST-NUMVAL-C(WS-EDIT-SIGNED-NUMBER-9V2-X) NOT = 0\n    SET FLG-SIGNED-NUMBER-NOT-OK TO TRUE
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Detailed_Business_Rule: Wrapper validator for US 10-digit phone numbers in 3-segment format (XXX) XXX-XXXX. Performs three sub-validators in sequence: EDIT-AREA-CODE for area code, EDIT-US-PHONE-PREFIX for prefix, EDIT-US-PHONE-LINENUM for line number.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM EDIT-AREA-CODE
   THRU EDIT-AREA-CODE-EXIT
PERFORM EDIT-US-PHONE-PREFIX
   THRU EDIT-US-PHONE-PREFIX-EXIT
PERFORM EDIT-US-PHONE-LINENUM
   THRU EDIT-US-PHONE-LINENUM-EXIT

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 3-digit US phone area code: must not be all spaces or zeros, must be numeric, must not start with 0 or 1, and must not match the reserved 555-nXX or 911 patterns. Comprehensive against North American Numbering Plan rules.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-A EQUAL SPACES
OR WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-A EQUAL ZEROES
OR WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-A IS NOT NUMERIC
    ...
    GO TO EDIT-AREA-CODE-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 3-digit US phone exchange (prefix) per the North American Numbering Plan: must be numeric, must not start with 0 or 1, and must not be 555-nXX in the directory-restricted range. Invalid prefixes raise an error flag and position the cursor on the phone-prefix field. The check is performed on every phone field on the customer record (home, work, mobile).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-B EQUAL SPACES
OR WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-B EQUAL ZEROES
    ...
    GO TO EDIT-US-PHONE-PREFIX-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 4-digit US phone line number: must be all 4 numeric digits and may not be all zeros. Invalid line numbers raise an error flag and position the cursor on the phone line-number field. This check complements the area-code and prefix validations to ensure a fully-formed 10-digit US phone number.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-C EQUAL SPACES
OR WS-EDIT-US-PHONE-NUM-C EQUAL ZEROES
    ...
    GO TO EDIT-US-PHONE-LINENUM-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 9-digit US Social Security Number per SSA format rules: 3-digit area number must not be 000, 666, or in 900-999 range; 2-digit group number must not be 00; 4-digit serial must not be 0000. Failure produces "SSN must be a valid 9 digit number."
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ACUP-NEW-CUST-SSN-X = SPACES OR ZEROES
    SET FLG-SSN-BLANK TO TRUE
ELSE
    IF ACUP-NEW-CUST-SSN-A < 1 OR
       ACUP-NEW-CUST-SSN-A = 666 OR
       ACUP-NEW-CUST-SSN-A > 899
        SET FLG-SSN-NOT-OK TO TRUE
    END-IF
END-IF
Review_Comments: PII (Social Security Number) compared and stored in plaintext - HIGH security risk; modernization target should mask, encrypt-at-rest, and use tokenization for non-display logic

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 2-character US state code by lookup against the VALID-STATE-2 table (50 states + DC). Failure produces "State must be a valid US state code."
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SEARCH VALID-STATE-2
    AT END
       SET FLG-STATE-NOT-OK TO TRUE
    WHEN VALID-STATE-2-CD(STATE-IDX) =
         WS-EDIT-ALPHANUM-ONLY(1:2)
       SET FLG-STATE-ISVALID TO TRUE
END-SEARCH
Review_Comments: Hardcoded US state code lookup table - externalize to configuration or database lookup; in cloud target use a reference data service

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the 3-digit FICO Credit Score is within the FICO range 300 to 850 inclusive. Out-of-range produces "FICO score must be between 300 and 850."
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IF ACUP-NEW-CUST-FICO-SCORE-N < 300\nOR ACUP-NEW-CUST-FICO-SCORE-N > 850\n    SET FLG-FICO-SCORE-NOT-OK TO TRUE
Review_Comments: Hardcoded FICO range 300-850 - externalize to config (although FICO range is industry standard, externalization eases regression testing and change-management)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Validates the cross-field consistency of US state code and US zip code: the first 3 digits of the zip code must fall within the prefix range valid for the selected state, looked up via the VALID-US-STATE-ZIP-CD2 table. On mismatch, an error is raised and the cursor is positioned at the zip code field. The lookup table is hardcoded in the program and must be maintained as USPS issues new ZIP prefixes.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SEARCH VALID-US-STATE-ZIP-CD2
    AT END
       SET FLG-STATE-ZIP-NOT-OK TO TRUE
    WHEN VALID-STATE-ZIP-CD2 = WS-VALIDATION-KEY
       SET FLG-STATE-ZIP-ISVALID TO TRUE
END-SEARCH
Review_Comments: Hardcoded US state-zip cross-reference table - externalize to reference data service / config table

Detailed_Business_Rule: When account details have not been fetched yet, perform the 9000-READ-ACCT lookup. If both account and customer are found, set ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS so the next iteration displays the data for editing.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED
WHEN CCARD-AID-PFK12
   IF  FLG-ACCTFILTER-ISVALID
       SET WS-RETURN-MSG-OFF       TO TRUE
       PERFORM 9000-READ-ACCT
          THRU 9000-READ-ACCT-EXIT
       IF FOUND-CUST-IN-MASTER
          SET ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS    TO TRUE
       END-IF
   END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: When details are shown and the user submits, if there are input edit errors or no changes detected, take no further action and redisplay the screen. Otherwise set ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED so the next iteration prompts the user for save confirmation via PF5. This is the two-phase commit pattern: validate first, then require explicit confirmation before persisting.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS
   IF INPUT-ERROR
   OR NO-CHANGES-DETECTED
      CONTINUE
   ELSE
      SET ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED TO TRUE
   END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: When details are shown but input edit errors were found, take no further action and redisplay the screen so the user can correct the errors. The error messages and field-level highlights persist on the redisplay. This branch is the inner loop of the validate-edit-resubmit cycle.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-NOT-OK\n    CONTINUE

Detailed_Business_Rule: When changes are pending confirmation and the user pressed PF5 to save, perform 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING. Then evaluate the outcome flags: lock failure -> ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-LOCK-ERROR; update failure -> ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-BUT-FAILED; record was changed by another user since lookup -> ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS for re-display; success -> ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-AND-DONE.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED
 AND CCARD-AID-PFK05
   PERFORM 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING
      THRU 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
   EVALUATE TRUE
      WHEN COULD-NOT-LOCK-ACCT-FOR-UPDATE
           SET ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-LOCK-ERROR TO TRUE
      WHEN LOCKED-BUT-UPDATE-FAILED
         SET ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-BUT-FAILED TO TRUE
      WHEN DATA-WAS-CHANGED-BEFORE-UPDATE
          SET ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS            TO TRUE
      WHEN OTHER
         SET ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-AND-DONE   TO TRUE
   END-EVALUATE
Review_Comments: Optimistic concurrency control via 9700-CHECK-CHANGE-IN-REC - convert to JPA @Version or DB row-level lock with retry

Detailed_Business_Rule: When changes are pending confirmation and the user pressed any key other than PF5 (e.g., Enter to revisit, or any other key), redisplay the screen with values still pending. The confirmation state is preserved so the user can review one more time before committing. Only PF5 advances to the actual save; other keys leave the user in the confirm-prompt state.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED\n    CONTINUE

Detailed_Business_Rule: After a successful update and confirmation cycle, set ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS to display the success and reset all search/context fields (account ID, card number, account status) when invoked standalone (not from a calling tranid). The reset clears the screen for the next account update, supporting back-to-back data-entry workflows. When invoked from another tranid, the search context is preserved so the calling program can resume.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-OKAYED-AND-DONE
    SET ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS TO TRUE
    IF CDEMO-FROM-TRANID    EQUAL LOW-VALUES
    OR CDEMO-FROM-TRANID    EQUAL SPACES
       MOVE ZEROES       TO CDEMO-ACCT-ID
                            CDEMO-CARD-NUM
       MOVE LOW-VALUES   TO CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS
    END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Unexpected control state — move ABEND-CULPRIT, code 0001, and message "UNEXPECTED DATA SCENARIO" then perform ABEND-ROUTINE to terminate the task with abend code 9999. This branch fires when none of the expected control flags are set, indicating a logic error or corrupted communication area. It is a defensive last-resort handler.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
     MOVE LIT-THISPGM    TO ABEND-CULPRIT
     MOVE '0001'         TO ABEND-CODE
     MOVE SPACES         TO ABEND-REASON
     MOVE 'UNEXPECTED DATA SCENARIO'
                         TO ABEND-MSG
     PERFORM ABEND-ROUTINE
        THRU ABEND-ROUTINE-EXIT
Review_Comments: Hardcoded abend code 0001 - replace with structured exception

Detailed_Business_Rule: Wrapper that orchestrates screen rendering: 3100 initialize header (date/time/title), 3200 set up data variables based on current state, 3250 prepare info message, 3300 set field attributes, 3390 set info message attributes, 3400 send. Each sub-paragraph has a single responsibility and is invoked in fixed order. This wrapper is the single entry point into the screen-output pipeline.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 3100-SCREEN-INIT
   THRU 3100-SCREEN-INIT-EXIT
PERFORM 3200-SETUP-SCREEN-VARS
   THRU 3200-SETUP-SCREEN-VARS-EXIT
PERFORM 3250-SETUP-INFOMSG
   THRU 3250-SETUP-INFOMSG-EXIT
PERFORM 3300-SETUP-SCREEN-ATTRS
   THRU 3300-SETUP-SCREEN-ATTRS-EXIT
PERFORM 3390-SETUP-INFOMSG-ATTRS
   THRU 3390-SETUP-INFOMSG-ATTRS-EXIT
PERFORM 3400-SEND-SCREEN
   THRU 3400-SEND-SCREEN-EXIT

Detailed_Business_Rule: Initializes the screen output area, captures current system date/time, and populates the screen header (title, transaction-id, program-name, current date MM/DD/YY, current time HH:MM:SS). The header is refreshed on every send so the user sees a current timestamp. Date/time formatting uses the standard COBOL intrinsic functions.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE LOW-VALUES TO CACTUPAO
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE     TO WS-CURDATE-DATA
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MONTH          TO WS-CURDATE-MM
MOVE WS-CURDATE-DAY            TO WS-CURDATE-DD
MOVE WS-CURDATE-YEAR(3:2)      TO WS-CURDATE-YY
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MM-DD-YY       TO CURDATEO OF CACTUPAO

Detailed_Business_Rule: Dispatcher: when context is ENTER, perform 3201 to show empty fields with prompt; when ACUP-DETAILS-FETCHED for the first time, perform 3202 to show original values; otherwise (re-edit), perform 3203 to show user-modified values that pass validation. This three-way branch handles the empty-screen, first-display, and re-edit states of the update workflow.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-PGM-ENTER
   PERFORM 3201-SHOW-INITIAL-VALUES
      THRU 3201-SHOW-INITIAL-VALUES-EXIT
ELSE
   IF ACUP-DETAILS-FETCHED-FIRST-TIME
      PERFORM 3202-SHOW-ORIGINAL-VALUES
         THRU 3202-SHOW-ORIGINAL-VALUES-EXIT
   ELSE
      PERFORM 3203-SHOW-UPDATED-VALUES
         THRU 3203-SHOW-UPDATED-VALUES-EXIT
   END-IF
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Empty edit screen: clears all 30+ output fields to spaces or low-values to prompt the user for the search key (Account ID). Only the Account ID field is unprotected; all other fields are read-only until details are fetched. This preserves the principle of least privilege on the edit form.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE SPACES TO ACCTSIDO OF CACTUPAO\nMOVE SPACES TO ACSTTUSO OF CACTUPAO\n...

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Populates output fields from the ACUP-OLD-* (just-read) structure: account ID, active status, balances, limits, dates, group ID; customer ID, names, address, phone numbers, SSN, DOB, EFT, primary card holder, FICO. This is the canonical first-display state — what the user sees immediately after fetching the record. All amounts are formatted from packed-decimal to display-friendly text.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE ACUP-OLD-ACCT-ID-X        TO ACCTSIDO OF CACTUPAO
MOVE ACUP-OLD-ACTIVE-STATUS    TO ACSTTUSO OF CACTUPAO
MOVE ACUP-OLD-CURR-BAL         TO ACURBALO OF CACTUPAO
...
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Detailed_Business_Rule: Populates output fields from the ACUP-NEW-* (user-edited) structure so the user sees their unsaved edits when the screen is redisplayed (e.g., after PF5 confirm prompt or edit error). This branch preserves the user's in-progress changes across pseudo-conversational round-trips. Saved values are retained until the user confirms or cancels.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID-X        TO ACCTSIDO OF CACTUPAO
MOVE ACUP-NEW-ACTIVE-STATUS    TO ACSTTUSO OF CACTUPAO
MOVE ACUP-NEW-CURR-BAL-X       TO ACURBALO OF CACTUPAO
...

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sets the user-facing info message based on the program state: "Enter or update id of account to display" for fresh entry, "Press PF5 to save changes, PF12 to cancel" for pending confirmation, "Account updated successfully" for success, "No changes detected" when user re-submits unchanged data. The message guides the user through each step of the workflow. Severity (color) is set separately by 3390-SETUP-INFOMSG-ATTRS.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED
         SET WS-PROMPT-FOR-INPUT TO TRUE
    WHEN ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED
         SET WS-INFORM-CONFIRM TO TRUE
    ...

Detailed_Business_Rule: Massive paragraph (~450 lines) that sets the BMS attribute byte and color for every input field. Uses a flag-driven decision tree to set red/default colors per validation flag (FLG-*-NOT-OK / FLG-*-ISVALID). Performs 3310-PROTECT-ALL-ATTRS on display screens and 3320-UNPROTECT-FEW-ATTRS only for the editable fields.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK
         MOVE DFHRED  TO ACCTSIDC OF CACTUPAO
    WHEN OTHER
         MOVE DFHDFCOL TO ACCTSIDC OF CACTUPAO
END-EVALUATE
(repeated for each field)

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sets the BMS protection attribute byte (DFHBMPRO) on every input field, making them read-only. Used when the screen is in display-only state (e.g., showing original values before user begins editing).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE DFHBMPRO  TO ACCTSIDA OF CACTUPAI\nMOVE DFHBMPRO  TO ACSTTUSA OF CACTUPAI\n...

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sets the BMS unprotect attribute on the subset of fields the user is allowed to edit, leaving read-only fields like dates and IDs protected. This enforces the edit-permission policy at the screen-rendering layer rather than relying on validation alone. Protected fields are visually distinguished in the 3270 rendering.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE DFHBMFSE  TO ACSTTUSA OF CACTUPAI\nMOVE DFHBMFSE  TO ACRDLIMA OF CACTUPAI\n...

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sets the color attribute on the info message line: dark for blank (no info), neutral for informational message, red for warning. This visual cue helps users distinguish prompts from errors at a glance. The color attribute is sent on every screen render even when the message text is unchanged.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF  WS-NO-INFO-MESSAGE
    MOVE DFHBMDAR           TO INFOMSGC OF CACTUPAO
ELSE
    MOVE DFHNEUTR           TO INFOMSGC OF CACTUPAO
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Sends the populated CACTUP map back to the user with CURSOR, ERASE, FREEKB and sets program context to REENTER for the next iteration. The CURSOR option positions the cursor on the field flagged as the next user-action target; ERASE clears the previous screen image; FREEKB unlocks the keyboard so the user can type. The REENTER flag tells the next iteration that this is not first-entry.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE LIT-THISMAPSET         TO CCARD-NEXT-MAPSET
MOVE LIT-THISMAP            TO CCARD-NEXT-MAP
SET  CDEMO-PGM-REENTER TO TRUE
EXEC CICS SEND MAP(CCARD-NEXT-MAP)
               MAPSET(CCARD-NEXT-MAPSET)
               FROM(CACTUPAO)
               CURSOR
               ERASE
               FREEKB
               RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Coordinator that fetches the cross-reference (9200), the account master (9300), the customer master (9400), and stores the fetched data as old-values (9500) for later comparison. Each step short-circuits on lookup failure.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
INITIALIZE ACUP-OLD-DETAILS
MOVE CC-ACCT-ID              TO ACUP-OLD-ACCT-ID
                                WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID
PERFORM 9200-GETCARDXREF-BYACCT
   THRU 9200-GETCARDXREF-BYACCT-EXIT
IF FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK
   GO TO 9000-READ-ACCT-EXIT
END-IF
PERFORM 9300-GETACCTDATA-BYACCT
   THRU 9300-GETACCTDATA-BYACCT-EXIT
...
Review_Comments: GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the Card Cross-Reference VSAM by account-ID alternate index. On success, copy the customer ID and card number into the commarea fields.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (LIT-CARDXREFNAME-ACCT-PATH)
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)
     ...
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL)
       MOVE XREF-CUST-ID     TO CDEMO-CUST-ID
       MOVE XREF-CARD-NUM    TO CDEMO-CARD-NUM

Detailed_Business_Rule: When no cross-reference record exists for the given account ID, set INPUT-ERROR and NOT-OK filter, build a diagnostic return message via STRING. The diagnostic embeds the account ID and response/reason codes so a support engineer can trace the failure. The user sees a generic "Account/Card relationship not found" message; the diagnostic is for the audit trail.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK       TO TRUE
    STRING 'Account:' WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X
    ' not found in Cross ref file.  Resp:' ERROR-RESP
    ' Reas:' ERROR-RESP2
    DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO WS-RETURN-MSG
    END-STRING

Detailed_Business_Rule: Generic file-error handler for non-NORMAL non-NOTFND responses. Builds the WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE template; SEND-LONG-TEXT call is commented out.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'READ'                     TO ERROR-OPNAME
    MOVE LIT-CARDXREFNAME-ACCT-PATH TO ERROR-FILE
    MOVE WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WS-RETURN-MSG
*    PERFORM SEND-LONG-TEXT
Review_Comments: Commented-out SEND-LONG-TEXT - error detail not logged for ops; replace with structured logging in cloud target

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the Account Master by account ID. On success, set FOUND-ACCT-IN-MASTER for downstream display logic.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (LIT-ACCTFILENAME)
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)
     INTO      (ACCOUNT-RECORD)
     ...
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL)
       SET FOUND-ACCT-IN-MASTER  TO TRUE
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: When the account master read returns NOTFND, set error flags and build a return message "Account:NN not found in Acct Master file. Resp:NN Reas:NN". The embedded codes help operations and support staff diagnose the failure. This is a soft error — the user can correct the Account ID and retry; no abend is raised.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-ACCTFILTER-NOT-OK       TO TRUE
    STRING 'Account:' WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X
    ' not found in Acct Master file.Resp:' ERROR-RESP
    ' Reas:' ERROR-RESP2
    DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO WS-RETURN-MSG
    END-STRING

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Generic file-error handler for non-NORMAL non-NOTFND on the account master read; copies WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE to WS-RETURN-MSG. SEND-LONG-TEXT commented out.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'READ'                     TO ERROR-OPNAME
    MOVE LIT-ACCTFILENAME           TO ERROR-FILE
    MOVE WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WS-RETURN-MSG
Review_Comments: Commented-out SEND-LONG-TEXT - error detail not logged

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Reads the Customer Master by customer ID. On success, set FOUND-CUST-IN-MASTER for downstream display logic.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (LIT-CUSTFILENAME)
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID-X)
     INTO      (CUSTOMER-RECORD)
     ...
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL)
       SET FOUND-CUST-IN-MASTER        TO TRUE

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: When the customer master read returns NOTFND, set error flags and build the return message "CustId:NN not found in customer master.Resp: NN REAS:NN". Indicates referential-integrity failure.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    SET INPUT-ERROR                 TO TRUE
    SET FLG-CUSTFILTER-NOT-OK       TO TRUE
    STRING 'CustId:' WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID-X
    ' not found in customer master.Resp: ' ERROR-RESP
    ' REAS:' ERROR-RESP2
    DELIMITED BY SIZE
    INTO WS-RETURN-MSG
    END-STRING
Review_Comments: Referential integrity gap - in cloud target enforce FK or cascade delete

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Generic file-error handler for non-NORMAL non-NOTFND on the customer master read. SEND-LONG-TEXT commented out.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'READ'                     TO ERROR-OPNAME
    MOVE LIT-CUSTFILENAME           TO ERROR-FILE
    MOVE WS-FILE-ERROR-MESSAGE      TO WS-RETURN-MSG
Review_Comments: Commented-out SEND-LONG-TEXT - error detail not logged

Detailed_Business_Rule: Stores the freshly-read account and customer data into the ACUP-OLD-* structure for later comparison and into the commarea for downstream use. Splits date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) into separate year, month, day sub-fields. Splits phone numbers stored as "(XXX)XXX-XXXX" into 3-segment editable form (area, prefix, line).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE ACCT-ID                   TO CDEMO-ACCT-ID
MOVE CUST-ID                   TO CDEMO-CUST-ID
MOVE CUST-FIRST-NAME           TO CDEMO-CUST-FNAME
...
(date and phone substring extraction)

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Re-reads the Account Master with UPDATE option to acquire a row-level lock. If the lock cannot be acquired (any non-NORMAL response), set COULD-NOT-LOCK-ACCT-FOR-UPDATE and skip to exit so the update is aborted.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     FILE      (LIT-ACCTFILENAME)
     UPDATE
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)
     KEYLENGTH (LENGTH OF WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X)
     INTO      (ACCOUNT-RECORD)
     LENGTH    (LENGTH OF ACCOUNT-RECORD)
     RESP      (WS-RESP-CD)
     RESP2     (WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC
IF WS-RESP-CD EQUAL TO DFHRESP(NORMAL)
   CONTINUE
ELSE
   SET COULD-NOT-LOCK-ACCT-FOR-UPDATE  TO TRUE
   GO TO 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Optimistic concurrency via VSAM record locking - convert to JPA @Version or DB row-level lock with retry; GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Input_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Re-reads the Customer Master with UPDATE option to acquire a second row-level lock. If lock fails, set COULD-NOT-LOCK-CUST-FOR-UPDATE and skip to exit. The earlier ACCTDAT lock is implicitly released only at task end - this is a 2-VSAM lock-then-update pattern with no explicit rollback at this stage.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     FILE      (LIT-CUSTFILENAME)
     UPDATE
     RIDFLD    (WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID-X)
     ...
END-EXEC
IF WS-RESP-CD EQUAL TO DFHRESP(NORMAL)
   CONTINUE
ELSE
   SET COULD-NOT-LOCK-CUST-FOR-UPDATE  TO TRUE
   GO TO 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Two-resource lock without saga or distributed-transaction guard - high risk of partial commit on second-lock failure; GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Detailed_Business_Rule: Performs 9700-CHECK-CHANGE-IN-REC to detect whether the freshly-read record differs from the old-values captured at lookup time (i.e., another transaction updated this record between lookup and update). If so, set DATA-WAS-CHANGED-BEFORE-UPDATE and skip to exit so the update is aborted and the screen redisplayed for user re-confirmation.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM 9700-CHECK-CHANGE-IN-REC
   THRU 9700-CHECK-CHANGE-IN-REC-EXIT
IF DATA-WAS-CHANGED-BEFORE-UPDATE
   GO TO 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Optimistic-lock detection via field-by-field re-read - replace with @Version timestamp/counter in JPA

Detailed_Business_Rule: Builds the ACCT-UPDATE-RECORD and CUST-UPDATE-RECORD from the edited ACUP-NEW-* fields. Reformats dates (CCYY/MM/DD) and phone numbers (XXX/YYY/ZZZZ) into the storage format. Copies all 30+ updated fields.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
INITIALIZE ACCT-UPDATE-RECORD
MOVE ACUP-NEW-ACCT-ID         TO ACCT-UPDATE-ID
MOVE ACUP-NEW-CURR-BAL-N      TO ACCT-UPDATE-CURR-BAL
MOVE ACUP-NEW-CREDIT-LIMIT-N  TO ACCT-UPDATE-CREDIT-LIMIT
...
STRING ACUP-NEW-OPEN-YEAR '-'
       ACUP-NEW-OPEN-MON '-'
       ACUP-NEW-OPEN-DAY
DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO ACCT-UPDATE-OPEN-DATE
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Output_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Rewrites the locked Account Master record. On non-NORMAL response, set LOCKED-BUT-UPDATE-FAILED and skip to exit. The customer-record rewrite is skipped on this failure path leaving the customer record locked but not updated until task-end implicit rollback.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS
     REWRITE FILE(LIT-ACCTFILENAME)
             FROM(ACCT-UPDATE-RECORD)
             LENGTH(LENGTH OF ACCT-UPDATE-RECORD)
             RESP      (WS-RESP-CD)
             RESP2     (WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC.
IF WS-RESP-CD EQUAL TO DFHRESP(NORMAL)
  CONTINUE
ELSE
  SET LOCKED-BUT-UPDATE-FAILED    TO TRUE
  GO TO 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Multi-file update without saga pattern - first REWRITE failure leaves locks held until task end; GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Output_File_Layout: CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CUST-FIRST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-MIDDLE-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-LAST-NAME(PIC X(25))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-1(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-2(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-LINE-3(PIC X(50))|CUST-ADDR-STATE-CD(PIC X(2))|CUST-ADDR-COUNTRY-CD(PIC X(3))|CUST-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-1(PIC X(15))|CUST-PHONE-NUM-2(PIC X(15))|CUST-SSN(PIC 9(9))|CUST-GOVT-ISSUED-ID(PIC X(20))|CUST-DOB-YYYY-MM-DD(PIC X(10))|CUST-EFT-ACCOUNT-ID(PIC X(10))|CUST-PRI-CARD-HOLDER-IND(PIC X(1))|CUST-FICO-CREDIT-SCORE(PIC 9(3))|FILLER(PIC X(168))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Rewrites the locked Customer Master record. On a non-NORMAL response, the program issues a synchronization-point rollback to undo the prior ACCTDAT rewrite (the only transaction boundary in the program), sets the LOCKED-BUT-UPDATE-FAILED state flag, and exits. This is the explicit two-resource transaction boundary protecting the account-customer pair: if either rewrite fails, both are reverted to maintain consistency.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS
             REWRITE FILE(LIT-CUSTFILENAME)
             FROM(CUST-UPDATE-RECORD)
             LENGTH(LENGTH OF CUST-UPDATE-RECORD)
             RESP      (WS-RESP-CD)
             RESP2     (WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC.
IF WS-RESP-CD EQUAL TO DFHRESP(NORMAL)
  CONTINUE
ELSE
  SET LOCKED-BUT-UPDATE-FAILED    TO TRUE
  EXEC CICS
     SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK
  END-EXEC
  GO TO 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING-EXIT
END-IF
Review_Comments: Two-resource VSAM update with explicit SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK - convert to declarative transaction (Spring @Transactional) with rollback-for in target architecture; GOTO statement - control flow that needs restructuring

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Compares the just-locked ACCTDAT and CUSTDAT record contents against the ACUP-OLD-* values captured at lookup time. If any field differs, set DATA-WAS-CHANGED-BEFORE-UPDATE so the update is aborted and the user is asked to re-review. This is the optimistic-concurrency check - it does not depend on version columns or timestamps but on field-level value comparison.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS         = ACUP-OLD-ACTIVE-STATUS
AND ACCT-CURR-BAL             = ACUP-OLD-CURR-BAL
AND ...
    CONTINUE
ELSE
    SET DATA-WAS-CHANGED-BEFORE-UPDATE TO TRUE
END-IF
Review_Comments: Field-by-field optimistic concurrency check - replace with @Version timestamp/counter in JPA or DB constraint

Detailed_Business_Rule: Abend handler invoked when an unhandled abend is intercepted by the registered abend trap. Defaults the abend message if none set, records this program as the culprit, sends the abend data area to the screen, cancels the abend trap so the next abend will not loop back, and raises a hardcoded abend code 9999 to terminate the task. This terminal handler is the program's last line of defense before the CICS region issues a default abend dump.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF ABEND-MSG EQUAL LOW-VALUES
   MOVE 'UNEXPECTED ABEND OCCURRED.' TO ABEND-MSG
END-IF
MOVE LIT-THISPGM       TO ABEND-CULPRIT
EXEC CICS SEND
         FROM (ABEND-DATA)
         LENGTH(LENGTH OF ABEND-DATA)
         NOHANDLE
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS HANDLE ABEND
     CANCEL
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS ABEND
     ABCODE('9999')
END-EXEC
Review_Comments: EXEC CICS ABEND with hardcoded code 9999 - replace with structured exception handling and observability emit

Detailed_Business_Rule: The COTRN00C program implements transaction CT00 - the Transaction List screen. It provides a paginated, filterable view of transactions from the TRANSACT VSAM KSDS file with 10 rows per page and PF7/PF8 navigation, with optional filter by Transaction ID.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.\nPROGRAM-ID. COTRN00C.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Initialize all transaction control flags to default state on every entry: error flag off, transaction file not at EOF, next-page-allowed yes, screen-erase yes. Clear the message buffer and force cursor on the Transaction ID input.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SET ERR-FLG-OFF TO TRUE
SET TRANSACT-NOT-EOF TO TRUE
SET NEXT-PAGE-NO TO TRUE
SET SEND-ERASE-YES TO TRUE
MOVE SPACES TO WS-MESSAGE
               ERRMSGO OF COTRN0AO
MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI

Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry with no commarea, route the user back to the signon screen via XCTL to COSGN00C. This guards against direct invocation of the transaction without prior login.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF EIBCALEN = 0
    MOVE 'COSGN00C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
    PERFORM RETURN-TO-PREV-SCREEN
ELSE
    ...
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry from another program, set re-enter flag, clear the screen output area, run PROCESS-ENTER-KEY (which triggers an initial page-forward fetch), and send the screen. This branch primes the transaction-list display with the first 10 transactions on cold-start. Subsequent iterations skip the primer and dispatch on the user's keystroke.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF NOT CDEMO-PGM-REENTER
    SET CDEMO-PGM-REENTER    TO TRUE
    MOVE LOW-VALUES          TO COTRN0AO
    PERFORM PROCESS-ENTER-KEY
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
ELSE
    PERFORM RECEIVE-TRNLST-SCREEN
    EVALUATE EIBAID
        ...

Detailed_Business_Rule: On Enter key, the user has either selected a transaction from the displayed list (S/s on a row) or entered a Tran ID filter. Run PROCESS-ENTER-KEY to validate and dispatch.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHENTER\n    PERFORM PROCESS-ENTER-KEY

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF3 key, return to the main menu (COMEN01C) via XCTL. PF3 is the standard 'exit' key in the application's UI convention.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHPF3\n    MOVE 'COMEN01C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM\n    PERFORM RETURN-TO-PREV-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF7 key, scroll the transaction list one page backward via PROCESS-PF7-KEY which positions on the first record of the current page and reads previous records. The result is the prior 10 transactions. If the user is already on page 1, the screen is redisplayed unchanged with a "Beginning of file" indicator.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHPF7\n    PERFORM PROCESS-PF7-KEY

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF8 key, scroll the transaction list one page forward via PROCESS-PF8-KEY which positions on the last record of the current page and reads next records. The result is the subsequent 10 transactions. If the end of the file is reached mid-page, the remaining rows are blank and a "End of file" indicator is set.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHPF8\n    PERFORM PROCESS-PF8-KEY

Detailed_Business_Rule: When any other key is pressed (PF1, PF2, PF4-PF6, etc.), set error flag, populate the message "Invalid key pressed." from the standard message constants (CCDA-MSG-INVALID-KEY), and re-send the screen. Only Enter, PF3, PF7, and PF8 are accepted on the transaction-list screen. This guard prevents users from triggering unintended behavior via unmapped function keys.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'Y'                       TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
    MOVE CCDA-MSG-INVALID-KEY      TO WS-MESSAGE
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Pseudo-conversational return with TRANSID=CT00 and the communication area preserved for the next iteration. The CICS region drops the program's working storage and resumes execution at this same program when the next user keystroke arrives. This is the core CardDemo state-passing mechanism for the transaction-list screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RETURN
          TRANSID (WS-TRANID)
          COMMAREA (CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC

Sub_Module: PROCESS-ENTER-KEY - WHEN SEL0001I OF COTRN0AI not blank
Detailed_Business_Rule: When the user has typed a selection character into the row 1 radio-button column, copy that character to the transaction selection flag and copy the displayed Tran ID from row 1 into the selection target. The selection flag is later evaluated for 'S' to dispatch to the detailed view.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN SEL0001I OF COTRN0AI NOT = SPACES AND LOW-VALUES\n    MOVE SEL0001I OF COTRN0AI TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SEL-FLG\n    MOVE TRNID01I OF COTRN0AI TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SELECTED

SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN SEL0007I OF COTRN0AI NOT = SPACES AND LOW-VALUES\n    MOVE SEL0007I OF COTRN0AI TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SEL-FLG\n    MOVE TRNID07I OF COTRN0AI TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SELECTED

Detailed_Business_Rule: When no row has a selection character entered, clear both the selection flag and the selected Tran ID to spaces. This branch handles the case where the user pressed Enter without selecting any transaction — the program redisplays the list without dispatching a detail-view action. The cleared state ensures no stale selection leaks across iterations.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN OTHER\n    MOVE SPACES   TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SEL-FLG\n    MOVE SPACES   TO CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SELECTED

Sub_Module: PROCESS-ENTER-KEY - IF SEL flag and SELECTED set
Detailed_Business_Rule: When both a selection flag and a Tran ID are populated, dispatch on selection character: 'S' or 's' (lower or upper case) means "View transaction detail" - perform XCTL to COTRN01C with the commarea passing the selected Tran ID. Note: COTRN01C is referenced but is NOT one of the programs registered by CBADMCDJ.jcl - it is registered separately.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF (CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SEL-FLG NOT = SPACES AND LOW-VALUES) AND
   (CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SELECTED NOT = SPACES AND LOW-VALUES)
    EVALUATE CDEMO-CT00-TRN-SEL-FLG
        WHEN 'S'
        WHEN 's'
             MOVE 'COTRN01C'   TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
             MOVE WS-TRANID    TO CDEMO-FROM-TRANID
             MOVE WS-PGMNAME   TO CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM
             MOVE 0        TO CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT
             EXEC CICS
                 XCTL PROGRAM(CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM)
                 COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
             END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: When a non-S character is entered as a selection (e.g., D, X, etc.), display "Invalid selection. Valid value is S" and reposition the cursor on the Tran ID filter. Note that the SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN call is commented out at this branch, so the message is set in WS-MESSAGE but the screen is not explicitly redisplayed - it falls through to the implicit fall-through path.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
*                SET TRANSACT-EOF TO TRUE
    MOVE
    'Invalid selection. Valid value is S' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
*                PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Commented-out SET TRANSACT-EOF and PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN - dead code or partial implementation

Detailed_Business_Rule: If a Tran ID filter is provided, it must be numeric. Blank filter means "start from beginning". Non-numeric input produces "Tran ID must be Numeric ..." error and the screen is re-sent without action.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF TRNIDINI OF COTRN0AI = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
    MOVE LOW-VALUES TO TRAN-ID
ELSE
    IF TRNIDINI  OF COTRN0AI IS NUMERIC
        MOVE TRNIDINI  OF COTRN0AI    TO TRAN-ID
    ELSE
        MOVE 'Y'                       TO WS-ERR-FLG
        MOVE
        'Tran ID must be Numeric ...' TO
                        WS-MESSAGE
        MOVE -1                 TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
        PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
    END-IF
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: After validation, reset page number to 0 and run the page-forward fetch to populate the screen. If no error occurred, clear the Tran ID input field on the output.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE 0       TO CDEMO-CT00-PAGE-NUM
PERFORM PROCESS-PAGE-FORWARD
IF NOT ERR-FLG-ON
    MOVE SPACE   TO TRNIDINO  OF COTRN0AO
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF7 (page back), set the file pointer to the first Tran ID currently on the page. If page number is greater than 1, perform PROCESS-PAGE-BACKWARD. Otherwise, display "You are already at the top of the page..." and re-send without erase.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-CT00-TRNID-FIRST = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
    MOVE LOW-VALUES TO TRAN-ID
ELSE
    MOVE CDEMO-CT00-TRNID-FIRST TO TRAN-ID
END-IF
SET NEXT-PAGE-YES TO TRUE
MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
IF CDEMO-CT00-PAGE-NUM > 1
    PERFORM PROCESS-PAGE-BACKWARD
ELSE
    MOVE 'You are already at the top of the page...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    SET SEND-ERASE-NO TO TRUE
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF8 (page forward), set the file pointer to the last Tran ID currently on the page (or HIGH-VALUES if blank). If next page is allowed, perform PROCESS-PAGE-FORWARD. Otherwise, display "You are already at the bottom of the page..." and re-send without erase.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-CT00-TRNID-LAST = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
    MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO TRAN-ID
ELSE
    MOVE CDEMO-CT00-TRNID-LAST TO TRAN-ID
END-IF
MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
IF NEXT-PAGE-YES
    PERFORM PROCESS-PAGE-FORWARD
ELSE
    MOVE 'You are already at the bottom of the page...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    SET SEND-ERASE-NO TO TRUE
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Page forward fetch: positions the browse cursor at the current TRAN-ID, optionally skips the first record if the user pressed Enter or PF7 (to avoid showing a duplicate top-of-page row), clears the 10 screen rows via INITIALIZE-TRAN-DATA, then reads the next 10 records sequentially calling POPULATE-TRAN-DATA for each. After populating, attempts to read 1 more record to detect end-of-file (sets NEXT-PAGE-YES if found, else NEXT-PAGE-NO), increments the page number, ends the browse, and sends the populated screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM STARTBR-TRANSACT-FILE
IF NOT ERR-FLG-ON
    IF EIBAID NOT = DFHENTER AND DFHPF7 AND DFHPF3
        PERFORM READNEXT-TRANSACT-FILE
    END-IF
    IF TRANSACT-NOT-EOF AND ERR-FLG-OFF
       PERFORM VARYING WS-IDX FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-IDX > 10
           PERFORM INITIALIZE-TRAN-DATA
       END-PERFORM
    END-IF
    MOVE 1             TO  WS-IDX
    PERFORM UNTIL WS-IDX >= 11 OR TRANSACT-EOF OR ERR-FLG-ON
        PERFORM READNEXT-TRANSACT-FILE
        IF TRANSACT-NOT-EOF AND ERR-FLG-OFF
            PERFORM POPULATE-TRAN-DATA
            COMPUTE WS-IDX = WS-IDX + 1
        END-IF
    END-PERFORM
    ...
    PERFORM ENDBR-TRANSACT-FILE
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
END-IF

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Page backward fetch: STARTBR positions cursor at TRAN-ID, skip first record if AID not Enter/PF8, INITIALIZE-TRAN-DATA, then loop READPREV up to 10 times calling POPULATE-TRAN-DATA for each. Decrement page number on success. Special handling to subtract 1 from page number rather than go below 1 - bounds check protects from underflow.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM STARTBR-TRANSACT-FILE
IF NOT ERR-FLG-ON
    IF EIBAID NOT = DFHENTER AND DFHPF8
        PERFORM READPREV-TRANSACT-FILE
    END-IF
    ...
    MOVE 10          TO  WS-IDX
    PERFORM UNTIL WS-IDX <= 0 OR TRANSACT-EOF OR ERR-FLG-ON
        PERFORM READPREV-TRANSACT-FILE
        IF TRANSACT-NOT-EOF AND ERR-FLG-OFF
            PERFORM POPULATE-TRAN-DATA
            COMPUTE WS-IDX = WS-IDX - 1
        END-IF
    END-PERFORM
    ...
    PERFORM ENDBR-TRANSACT-FILE
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Top of POPULATE-TRAN-DATA: decompose the TRAN-ORIG-TS timestamp string ("YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.SSSSSS") into MM-DD-YY display format and copy the transaction amount to the display variable. This formatting runs once per record.

MOVE TRAN-AMT                  TO WS-TRAN-AMT
MOVE TRAN-ORIG-TS              TO WS-TIMESTAMP
MOVE WS-TIMESTAMP-DT-YYYY(3:2) TO WS-CURDATE-YY
MOVE WS-TIMESTAMP-DT-MM        TO WS-CURDATE-MM
MOVE WS-TIMESTAMP-DT-DD        TO WS-CURDATE-DD
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MM-DD-YY       TO WS-TRAN-DATE
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

    MOVE SPACES TO TDESC04I OF COTRN0AI
    MOVE SPACES TO TAMT004I OF COTRN0AI

SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN 5
    MOVE SPACES TO TRNID05I OF COTRN0AI
    MOVE SPACES TO TDATE05I OF COTRN0AI
    MOVE SPACES TO TDESC05I OF COTRN0AI
    MOVE SPACES TO TAMT005I OF COTRN0AI

    MOVE SPACES TO TDATE09I OF COTRN0AI

Detailed_Business_Rule: Defensive default catch-all branch — silently continues with no action. The branch exists to satisfy COBOL case-statement syntax requiring an exhaustive case list. Reaching this branch would indicate an unexpected row-counter value, which is a logic error rather than an expected runtime condition. The silent continue is itself a modernization risk and should become a logged exception in Java.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN OTHER\n    CONTINUE

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Return to a calling program (caller specified in CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM, defaults to COSGN00C signon if blank) via control-transfer with the communication area preserving context. This is the user-initiated exit path triggered by PF3. The calling program receives back the same communication area it had passed in, so the user's session state is preserved across the round-trip.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM = LOW-VALUES OR SPACES
    MOVE 'COSGN00C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
END-IF
MOVE WS-TRANID    TO CDEMO-FROM-TRANID
MOVE WS-PGMNAME   TO CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM
MOVE ZEROS        TO CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT
EXEC CICS
    XCTL PROGRAM(CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM)
    COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: Performs POPULATE-HEADER-INFO to set the title bar and current date/time, copies WS-MESSAGE to ERRMSGO, then sends the COTRN0A map. Branches on SEND-ERASE-YES/NO: ERASE clears any prior screen content; no-erase preserves the existing screen and overlays only the changed rows.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM POPULATE-HEADER-INFO
MOVE WS-MESSAGE TO ERRMSGO OF COTRN0AO
IF SEND-ERASE-YES
    EXEC CICS SEND
              MAP('COTRN0A')
              MAPSET('COTRN00')
              FROM(COTRN0AO)
              ERASE
              CURSOR
    END-EXEC
ELSE
    EXEC CICS SEND ... (without ERASE)
END-IF

Detailed_Business_Rule: Receive the COTRN0A map into the COTRN0AI input area; response and reason codes are captured for downstream error handling. If the user pressed CLEAR or no map data was sent, the receive returns a MAPFAIL response and the program redisplays the screen unchanged. This is the standard CICS BMS receive idiom.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RECEIVE
          MAP('COTRN0A')
          MAPSET('COTRN00')
          INTO(COTRN0AI)
          RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
          RESP2(WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Populate the screen header: title lines (CCDA-TITLE01/02 constants), transaction-id, program-name, current date (MM/DD/YY), current time (HH:MM:SS) using the COBOL CURRENT-DATE intrinsic function. The header is refreshed on every send so the user sees a current timestamp. The date and time displays use program-local formatting independent of the BMS map definitions.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE  TO WS-CURDATE-DATA
MOVE CCDA-TITLE01           TO TITLE01O OF COTRN0AO
MOVE CCDA-TITLE02           TO TITLE02O OF COTRN0AO
MOVE WS-TRANID              TO TRNNAMEO OF COTRN0AO
MOVE WS-PGMNAME             TO PGMNAMEO OF COTRN0AO
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MONTH       TO WS-CURDATE-MM
...
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MM-DD-YY    TO CURDATEO OF COTRN0AO
...
MOVE WS-CURTIME-HH-MM-SS    TO CURTIMEO OF COTRN0AO

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Establish a browse positioning on TRANSACT VSAM by Tran ID. Note the GTEQ option is COMMENTED OUT - the browse uses exact key positioning, which means a key not exactly matching a record produces NOTFND.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS STARTBR
     DATASET   (WS-TRANSACT-FILE)
     RIDFLD    (TRAN-ID)
     KEYLENGTH (LENGTH OF TRAN-ID)
*         GTEQ
     RESP      (WS-RESP-CD)
     RESP2     (WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL)
        CONTINUE
Review_Comments: Commented-out GTEQ option means exact key match required - may cause unexpected NOTFND when key is partial; review for correct behavior in target architecture

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: When STARTBR returns NOTFND, the program executes a CONTINUE statement BEFORE the SET TRANSACT-EOF and message lines. The CONTINUE causes the entire NOTFND branch to be a no-op silent failure: the SET, MOVEs, and subsequent invocations after CONTINUE are dead code that is never reached. This is a CRITICAL silent-failure bug.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    CONTINUE
    SET TRANSACT-EOF TO TRUE
    MOVE 'You are at the top of the page...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
Review_Comments: BUG: CONTINUE executed BEFORE SET TRANSACT-EOF - all subsequent statements are dead code; CONTINUE after non-zero SQLCODE / RESP - silent failures that must become logged exceptions in Java/Glue

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: On any other STARTBR response code, write the response/reason codes to the system output stream for operations diagnostics, set the error flag, populate the message "Unable to lookup transaction...", and re-send the screen. This branch handles I/O errors, file-unavailable conditions, and other non-NORMAL non-NOTFND responses. The DISPLAY to SYSOUT pattern is a modernization risk (see Review_Comments).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    DISPLAY 'RESP:' WS-RESP-CD 'REAS:' WS-REAS-CD
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Unable to lookup transaction...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO TRNIDINL OF COTRN0AI
    PERFORM SEND-TRNLST-SCREEN
Review_Comments: DISPLAY to SYSOUT - replace with structured logging (SLF4J/Log4j2) in target architecture

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Read the next record in the TRANSACT browse. The normal-response branch continues. The end-of-file branch has the same silent-failure pattern as STARTBR's NOTFND (CONTINUE before SET TRANSACT-EOF), so the EOF detection is bug-prone. The default catch-all branch displays response codes, sets the error flag, and shows the error message.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READNEXT
     DATASET (WS-TRANSACT-FILE)
     INTO (TRAN-RECORD)
     ...
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL) CONTINUE
    WHEN DFHRESP(ENDFILE)
        CONTINUE
        SET TRANSACT-EOF TO TRUE
        MOVE 'You have reached the bottom...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    WHEN OTHER
        DISPLAY 'RESP:' WS-RESP-CD 'REAS:' WS-REAS-CD
        MOVE 'Y' TO WS-ERR-FLG
Review_Comments: BUG: CONTINUE before SET TRANSACT-EOF in ENDFILE branch - subsequent statements are dead code; CONTINUE after non-zero SQLCODE / RESP - silent failures that must become logged exceptions in Java/Glue; DISPLAY to SYSOUT - replace with structured logging

Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Read the previous record in the TRANSACT browse (used for PF7 page-back). Same WHEN NORMAL/ENDFILE/OTHER pattern as READNEXT, with the same silent-failure CONTINUE-before-SET bug in the ENDFILE branch.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READPREV
     DATASET (WS-TRANSACT-FILE)
     INTO (TRAN-RECORD)
     ...
END-EXEC
EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD
    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL) CONTINUE
    WHEN DFHRESP(ENDFILE)
        CONTINUE
        SET TRANSACT-EOF TO TRUE
        MOVE 'You have reached the top...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    WHEN OTHER
        DISPLAY 'RESP:' WS-RESP-CD 'REAS:' WS-REAS-CD
Review_Comments: BUG: CONTINUE before SET TRANSACT-EOF in ENDFILE branch - subsequent statements are dead code; CONTINUE after non-zero SQLCODE / RESP - silent failures that must become logged exceptions in Java/Glue

Detailed_Business_Rule: End the active browse on the TRANSACT VSAM file, releasing any positioning held by STARTBR. No response code check is performed - relies on CICS task termination to clean up if this fails.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: EXEC CICS ENDBR\n     DATASET   (WS-TRANSACT-FILE)\nEND-EXEC
Review_Comments: ENDBR response code not checked - in target architecture wrap in try-finally with explicit close handler

Detailed_Business_Rule: The COBIL00C program implements transaction CB00 - the Bill Payment screen. The user enters an Account ID and confirms (Y/N) to apply a full-balance bill payment, which generates a TRANSACT record with type 02 / category 2 (debit), and decrements ACCT-CURR-BAL by the payment amount.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.\nPROGRAM-ID. COBIL00C.

Detailed_Business_Rule: Reset error and user-modified flags on every entry; clear the message buffer. This initialization runs at the top of MAIN-PARA before any branching so that stale flags from a prior pseudo-conversational iteration do not influence current logic. The clean-slate guarantee is essential for predictable behavior.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
SET ERR-FLG-OFF     TO TRUE
SET USR-MODIFIED-NO TO TRUE
MOVE SPACES TO WS-MESSAGE
               ERRMSGO OF COBIL0AO

Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time entry without a communication area (no commarea passed in), route to the signon program (COSGN00C) to force the user to authenticate before accessing bill-payment functionality. This guards against direct invocation of the transaction without prior login. The check is a security boundary at every program entry point.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: IF EIBCALEN = 0\n    MOVE 'COSGN00C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM\n    PERFORM RETURN-TO-PREV-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Sub_Module: MAIN-PARA - IF NOT REENTER + pre-populate from selection
Detailed_Business_Rule: On first-time fresh entry, set re-enter flag, clear screen output, focus cursor on Account ID. If a transaction was pre-selected by an upstream caller (CDEMO-CB00-TRN-SELECTED is populated), copy it into the input area and run PROCESS-ENTER-KEY to auto-run the payment process.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF NOT CDEMO-PGM-REENTER
    SET CDEMO-PGM-REENTER    TO TRUE
    MOVE LOW-VALUES          TO COBIL0AO
    MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI
    IF CDEMO-CB00-TRN-SELECTED NOT =
                               SPACES AND LOW-VALUES
        MOVE CDEMO-CB00-TRN-SELECTED TO
             ACTIDINI OF COBIL0AI
        PERFORM PROCESS-ENTER-KEY
    END-IF
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: On Enter, run PROCESS-ENTER-KEY which validates input, looks up the account, and conditionally generates the bill-payment transaction. The action depends on the user's confirmation flag value: Y processes the payment, N declines, blank prompts for confirmation. This is the central business-logic dispatcher for the bill-payment workflow.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHENTER\n    PERFORM PROCESS-ENTER-KEY

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF3 (exit), return to the caller program if known (CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM was set by the upstream tranid), or to the main menu COMEN01C if unknown. This honors the bidirec-tional caller-callee linkage convention.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHPF3
    IF CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
        MOVE 'COMEN01C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
    ELSE
        MOVE CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM TO
        CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
    END-IF
    PERFORM RETURN-TO-PREV-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: On PF4, clear all input fields by performing CLEAR-CURRENT-SCREEN (which initializes all fields and re-sends the screen). This is the user-initiated reset path before any commit, equivalent to starting over on the same screen. Any unsaved input is destroyed; the user is back to a clean slate.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN DFHPF4\n    PERFORM CLEAR-CURRENT-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: When any other key is pressed, set error flag, populate the standard "Invalid key pressed." message, and re-send the screen. Only Enter, PF3, and PF4 are accepted on the bill-payment screen. This guard prevents users from triggering unintended actions via unmapped function keys.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'Y'                       TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE CCDA-MSG-INVALID-KEY      TO WS-MESSAGE
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Pseudo-conversational return with TRANSID=CB00 and the communication area preserved for the next iteration. The CICS region drops the program's working storage and resumes execution at this same program when the next user keystroke arrives. This is the core CardDemo state-passing mechanism for the bill-payment screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RETURN
          TRANSID (WS-TRANID)
          COMMAREA (CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC

Sub_Module: PROCESS-ENTER-KEY - validate Acct ID not blank
Detailed_Business_Rule: First validation: account ID must not be blank. If blank, set error flag, populate "Acct ID can NOT be empty...", force cursor on the field, and re-send the screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN ACTIDINI OF COBIL0AI = SPACES OR LOW-VALUES
        MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
        MOVE 'Acct ID can NOT be empty...' TO
                        WS-MESSAGE
        MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI
        PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN
    WHEN OTHER
        CONTINUE
END-EVALUATE

Detailed_Business_Rule: When user enters Y/y in the confirmation field, set CONF-PAY-YES and read the account master via READ-ACCTDAT-FILE. The lookup returns either the account record (success) or an error (NOTFND/file error - handled within READ-ACCTDAT-FILE).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EVALUATE CONFIRMI OF COBIL0AI
    WHEN 'Y'
    WHEN 'y'
        SET CONF-PAY-YES TO TRUE
        PERFORM READ-ACCTDAT-FILE

Detailed_Business_Rule: When user enters N or n, decline the payment — clear all fields and set the error flag to short-circuit downstream logic. The screen is redisplayed in a clean state ready for a new entry. The decline is a non-destructive cancel — no transaction is written.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN 'N'
WHEN 'n'
    PERFORM CLEAR-CURRENT-SCREEN
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG

Detailed_Business_Rule: When confirmation field is blank, just look up the account to display the current balance and prompt user to confirm. No transaction is generated yet.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: WHEN SPACES\nWHEN LOW-VALUES\n    PERFORM READ-ACCTDAT-FILE

Detailed_Business_Rule: When confirm field is non-blank and not Y/N, error "Invalid value. Valid values are (Y/N)..." and re-send screen with cursor on confirm field.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN OTHER
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Invalid value. Valid values are (Y/N)...'
                 TO WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1      TO CONFIRML OF COBIL0AI
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: After the account read, copy the current balance to the output display field for the user to see before confirming the payment. The balance is formatted from packed decimal to a display-friendly amount with two decimal places. This is the visual confirmation that the user is paying against the correct account.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements: MOVE ACCT-CURR-BAL TO WS-CURR-BAL\nMOVE WS-CURR-BAL   TO CURBALI    OF COBIL0AI
Review_Comments: Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Detailed_Business_Rule: Business rule: cannot pay a non-positive balance. If account balance is zero or negative AND account ID was supplied, display "You have nothing to pay..." and re-send screen.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF NOT ERR-FLG-ON
    IF ACCT-CURR-BAL <= ZEROS AND
       ACTIDINI OF COBIL0AI NOT = SPACES AND LOW-VALUES
        MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
        MOVE 'You have nothing to pay...' TO
                        WS-MESSAGE
        MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI
        PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN
    END-IF
END-IF

Sub_Module: PROCESS-ENTER-KEY - Generate Tran ID via STARTBR/READPREV
Input_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Generate the next transaction ID by browsing TRANSACT in reverse from HIGH-VALUES to find the maximum existing ID, then add 1. This is application-managed key generation - a well-known anti-pattern with race-condition risk under concurrent load (two transactions could generate the same ID). Modernization target should use a database SEQUENCE or UUID generation.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM READ-CXACAIX-FILE
MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO TRAN-ID
PERFORM STARTBR-TRANSACT-FILE
PERFORM READPREV-TRANSACT-FILE
PERFORM ENDBR-TRANSACT-FILE
MOVE TRAN-ID     TO WS-TRAN-ID-NUM
ADD 1 TO WS-TRAN-ID-NUM
Review_Comments: Application-managed key generation via STARTBR/READPREV (find max+1) - race condition risk in concurrent transactions; replace with database SEQUENCE or UUID generation

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Initialize the new TRAN-RECORD with: generated Tran ID, type 02 (debit), category 2, source 'POS TERM', description 'BILL PAYMENT - ONLINE', amount equal to current account balance, card number from the cross-reference, hardcoded merchant ID 999999999 with name 'BILL PAYMENT' and city/zip 'N/A'. These hardcoded values represent the system as the merchant of record for bill payments.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
INITIALIZE TRAN-RECORD
MOVE WS-TRAN-ID-NUM       TO TRAN-ID
MOVE '02'                 TO TRAN-TYPE-CD
MOVE 2                    TO TRAN-CAT-CD
MOVE 'POS TERM'           TO TRAN-SOURCE
MOVE 'BILL PAYMENT - ONLINE' TO TRAN-DESC
MOVE ACCT-CURR-BAL        TO TRAN-AMT
MOVE XREF-CARD-NUM        TO TRAN-CARD-NUM
MOVE 999999999            TO TRAN-MERCHANT-ID
MOVE 'BILL PAYMENT'       TO TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME
MOVE 'N/A'                TO TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY
MOVE 'N/A'                TO TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP
Review_Comments: Hardcoded business rule values (TRAN-TYPE-CD, CAT-CD, SOURCE, DESC, MERCHANT-ID, MERCHANT-NAME, MERCHANT-CITY/ZIP) - externalize to configuration table or properties file

Output_File_Layout: TRAN-ID(PIC X(16))|TRAN-TYPE-CD(PIC X(2))|TRAN-CAT-CD(PIC 9(4))|TRAN-SOURCE(PIC X(10))|TRAN-DESC(PIC X(100))|TRAN-AMT(PIC S9(9)V99)|TRAN-MERCHANT-ID(PIC 9(9))|TRAN-MERCHANT-NAME(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-CITY(PIC X(50))|TRAN-MERCHANT-ZIP(PIC X(10))|TRAN-CARD-NUM(PIC X(16))|TRAN-ORIG-TS(PIC X(26))|TRAN-PROC-TS(PIC X(26))|FILLER(PIC X(20)) (TRANSACT) | ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178)) (ACCTDAT)
Detailed_Business_Rule: Capture the current timestamp from CICS, set both origination and processing timestamps to the same value, write the new transaction record, decrement the account balance by the transaction amount, and update the account record. This is the multi-file dual-write sequence with NO explicit SYNCPOINT - relies on CICS task-end implicit commit. If the WRITE succeeds but the REWRITE fails, the transaction exists but the balance is not decremented (data inconsis-tency risk).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM GET-CURRENT-TIMESTAMP
MOVE WS-TIMESTAMP         TO TRAN-ORIG-TS
                             TRAN-PROC-TS
PERFORM WRITE-TRANSACT-FILE
COMPUTE ACCT-CURR-BAL = ACCT-CURR-BAL - TRAN-AMT
PERFORM UPDATE-ACCTDAT-FILE
Review_Comments: Multi-file dual-write without explicit SYNCPOINT - high risk of partial commit on REWRITE failure leaving TRANSACT row without matching ACCTDAT update; introduce explicit SYNCPOINT or use distributed transaction (Saga pattern); Packed-decimal (COMP-3) arithmetic - map to BigDecimal in Java or Decimal in Spark

Detailed_Business_Rule: When confirmation is not yet given (CONF-PAY-NO), display "Confirm to make a bill payment..." and force cursor on the confirmation field. The screen then awaits user Y/N.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
ELSE
    MOVE 'Confirm to make a bill payment...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO CONFIRML OF COBIL0AI
END-IF
PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: Captures the current system time, then formats it into a calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) and clock time (HH:MM:SS). Concatenates into a 26-byte timestamp "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" with milliseconds defaulted to zeros — microsecond precision is lost, so all transaction timestamps from this program end in .000000. The timestamp drives the TRAN-PROC-TS field on every payment transaction record written by this program.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS ASKTIME
  ABSTIME(WS-ABS-TIME)
END-EXEC
EXEC CICS FORMATTIME
  ABSTIME(WS-ABS-TIME)
  YYYYMMDD(WS-CUR-DATE-X10)
  DATESEP('-')
  TIME(WS-CUR-TIME-X08)
  TIMESEP(':')
END-EXEC
INITIALIZE WS-TIMESTAMP
MOVE WS-CUR-DATE-X10 TO WS-TIMESTAMP(01:10)
MOVE WS-CUR-TIME-X08 TO WS-TIMESTAMP(12:08)
MOVE ZEROS           TO WS-TIMESTAMP-TM-MS6
Review_Comments: Timestamp microseconds always zero - in cloud target use Instant.now() with full precision

Output_File_Layout: CDEMO-FROM-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-TO-TRANID(PIC X(4))|CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-ID(PIC X(8))|CDEMO-USER-TYPE(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT(PIC 9(1))|CDEMO-CUST-ID(PIC 9(9))|CDEMO-CUST-FNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-MNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-CUST-LNAME(PIC X(25))|CDEMO-ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|CDEMO-ACCT-STATUS(PIC X(1))|CDEMO-CARD-NUM(PIC 9(16))|CDEMO-LAST-MAP(PIC X(7))|CDEMO-LAST-MAPSET(PIC X(7))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Return to a calling program (default COSGN00C if the calling-program field is blank) via control-transfer with the communication area preserving context. This is the user-initiated exit path triggered by PF3. The calling program receives back the same communication area it had passed in, so the user's session state is preserved across the round-trip.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
IF CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM = LOW-VALUES OR SPACES
    MOVE 'COSGN00C' TO CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM
END-IF
MOVE WS-TRANID    TO CDEMO-FROM-TRANID
MOVE WS-PGMNAME   TO CDEMO-FROM-PROGRAM
MOVE ZEROS        TO CDEMO-PGM-CONTEXT
EXEC CICS
    XCTL PROGRAM(CDEMO-TO-PROGRAM)
    COMMAREA(CARDDEMO-COMMAREA)
END-EXEC
Review_Comments: Multi-program CALL chain via XCTL - identify as microservice or Step Function boundary

Detailed_Business_Rule: Populate the title bar header (POPULATE-HEADER-INFO), copy any pending message into the error-message line, and send the COBIL0A map with ERASE+CURSOR for fresh display. The ERASE clears the previous screen image and the CURSOR option positions the cursor on the next user-action target. This is the canonical bill-payment screen render.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
PERFORM POPULATE-HEADER-INFO
MOVE WS-MESSAGE TO ERRMSGO OF COBIL0AO
EXEC CICS SEND
          MAP('COBIL0A')
          MAPSET('COBIL00')
          FROM(COBIL0AO)
          ERASE
          CURSOR
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Receive the COBIL0A map into the COBIL0AI input area; response and reason codes are captured for downstream error handling. If the user pressed CLEAR or no map data was sent, the receive returns a MAPFAIL response and the program redisplays the screen unchanged. This is the standard CICS BMS receive idiom.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS RECEIVE
          MAP('COBIL0A')
          MAPSET('COBIL00')
          INTO(COBIL0AI)
          RESP(WS-RESP-CD)
          RESP2(WS-REAS-CD)
END-EXEC

Detailed_Business_Rule: Populate the screen title bar with system constants and the current date/time using the COBOL CURRENT-DATE intrinsic function. The header is refreshed on every send so the user sees a current timestamp. The constants are sourced from the shared COTTL01Y copybook so the title bar is consistent across programs.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE  TO WS-CURDATE-DATA
MOVE CCDA-TITLE01           TO TITLE01O OF COBIL0AO
MOVE CCDA-TITLE02           TO TITLE02O OF COBIL0AO
MOVE WS-TRANID              TO TRNNAMEO OF COBIL0AO
MOVE WS-PGMNAME             TO PGMNAMEO OF COBIL0AO
MOVE WS-CURDATE-MM-DD-YY    TO CURDATEO OF COBIL0AO
MOVE WS-CURTIME-HH-MM-SS    TO CURTIMEO OF COBIL0AO

Input_File_Layout: ACCT-ID(PIC 9(11))|ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS(PIC X(1))|ACCT-CURR-BAL(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-OPEN-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-EXPIRAION-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-REISSUE-DATE(PIC X(10))|ACCT-CURR-CYC-CREDIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-CURR-CYC-DEBIT(PIC S9(10)V99)|ACCT-ADDR-ZIP(PIC X(10))|ACCT-GROUP-ID(PIC X(10))|FILLER(PIC X(178))
Detailed_Business_Rule: Read the Account Master with UPDATE option to acquire a row-level lock for the upcoming REWRITE. WHEN NORMAL continues to next step (downstream balance display + payment processing).
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
EXEC CICS READ
     DATASET   (WS-ACCTDAT-FILE)
     INTO      (ACCOUNT-RECORD)
     LENGTH    (LENGTH OF ACCOUNT-RECORD)
     RIDFLD    (ACCT-ID)
     KEYLENGTH (LENGTH OF ACCT-ID)
     UPDATE

    WHEN DFHRESP(NORMAL)
        CONTINUE

Detailed_Business_Rule: When the account ID is not found in the Account Master, set the error flag, populate the message "Account ID NOT found...", focus the cursor on the Account ID field, and re-send the screen. This is a soft error — the user can correct the Account ID and retry. No abend is raised; no transaction is written.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Account ID NOT found...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: On other response codes (e.g., I/O error, file unavailable), write the response/reason codes to the system output stream for operations diagnostics, raise the error "Unable to lookup Account...", and re-send the screen. This branch handles unexpected file conditions distinct from NOTFND. The DISPLAY to SYSOUT pattern is a modernization risk (see Review_Comments).

    DISPLAY 'RESP:' WS-RESP-CD 'REAS:' WS-REAS-CD

    MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN

Detailed_Business_Rule: The not-found branch should not occur on a REWRITE after a successful READ FOR UPDATE, but if it does, errors with "Account ID NOT found...". The default catch-all branch displays response/reason codes and errors with "Unable to Update Account...". Note: this REWRITE failure leaves the TRANSACT WRITE committed (no SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK) - data inconsistency between transaction record and account balance.
SQL_Decision_Control_Statements:
WHEN DFHRESP(NOTFND)
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Account ID NOT found...' TO WS-MESSAGE
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN
WHEN OTHER
    DISPLAY 'RESP:' WS-RESP-CD 'REAS:' WS-REAS-CD
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG
    MOVE 'Unable to Update Account...' TO
                    WS-MESSAGE
    PERFORM SEND-BILLPAY-SCREEN
Review_Comments: Multi-file dual-write inconsistency: TRANSACT write committed but ACCTDAT REWRITE failed - introduce explicit SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK or use distributed transaction (Saga); DISPLAY to SYSOUT - replace with structured logging

EXEC CICS READ

EVALUATE WS-RESP-CD

    WHEN OTHER ...

WHEN DFHRESP(DUPREC)
    MOVE 'Y'     TO WS-ERR-FLG

    MOVE -1       TO ACTIDINL OF COBIL0AI

This is the screen-clear path invoked from PF4 and from the post-decline reset. The user is returned to a blank Account ID prompt ready for a new entry.
